title: Product Manager (Contact Centre) - Engine by Starling
description_text:
At Engine by Starling, we are on a mission to find and work with leading banks all around the world who have the ambition to build rapid growth businesses, on our technology. 
Engine is Starling's software-as-a-service (SaaS) business, the technology that was built to power Starling Bank, and two years ago we split out as a separate business. 
Starling has seen exceptional growth and success, and a large part of that is down to the fact that we have built our own modern technology from the ground up. This SaaS technology platform is now available to banks and financial institutions all around the world, enabling them to benefit from the innovative digital features, and efficient back-office processes that has helped achieve Starling's success.
We draw upon our experience as knowledgeable bankers, and best in class technologists to become the chosen option for these banks, and preferred partners for leading consultancies.
Hybrid Working
We have a Hybrid approach to working here at Engine - our preference is that you're located within a commutable distance of one of our offices so that we're able to interact and collaborate in person.
About the role
This role is pivotal within Engine’s Product function, in making sure we understand the client needs, sell the right thing, and build the right product. We’re looking for a specialist who is passionate about Customer Service to own and shape our built-in Contact Centre capabilities. 
We’re looking for versatile, adaptable individuals who enjoy the challenge of a varied and collaborative role. You’ll work closely with the Business Development, Customer Success and Engineering functions to develop and deliver against the Contact Centre roadmap and domain strategy, as well as acting as a Contact Centre SME across the business. 
You’ll enjoy problem solving, getting to the detail, and understanding how clients can make the best use of our product, whilst designing ways to improve it. 
What you'll get to do
Take ownership of the Contact Centre product domain owning the end-to-end discovery, design, and delivery of new Contact Centre features.
Define the Contact Centre domain strategy and Roadmap, shaping our future plans for this critical part of our offering.
You’ll work cross-functionally with engineers, designers, document writers, and QA to drive the design and delivery of improvements to the product that align with the vision of the business, taking into account existing and potential clients.
You’ll confidently and independently prioritise incoming tasks and clearly communicate ‘the what’ and ‘the why’ of each decision to both internal and external stakeholders. 
You’ll work with the Engineering Lead in defining the Engineering plan for the domain, collaborating to ensure delivery remains on track and proactively raising risks that may impact our client projects.
You’ll develop a close relationship with our Delivery and Customer Success teams, taking the lead on assessing feature requests and client requirements as a key input into overall feature and roadmap definition.
You’ll work closely with our Business Development team by helping the team understand Engine’s capabilities in your domain and joining early-stage Sales conversations as an SME. 
You’ll become the internal champion for Engine’s Contact Centre, developing materials and messaging for use by the rest of the business. 
You’ll enjoy problem solving, getting to the detail, and understanding how clients can make the best use of our product, whilst designing ways to improve it. Your work will span product strategy, client-facing requirements gathering & solution design, and product ownership in an Engineering context.
Hear more from the team in some case studies, below, and our work with Women In Tech.
Product in Practice 
Your background
4+ years of direct Product manager/owner experience, ideally in FinTech or a business using modern Product development & Engineering techniques.
You have relevant experience in Customer Experience/Contact Centre, a passion for service generally, and a good understanding of the Contact Centre technology offerings in the market and their relative strengths and weaknesses. 
You have owned and delivered new features, projects or products from start to finish including post-launch maintenance and improvement. 
You have experience working directly with APIs.
You have experience of rapidly understanding problems and presenting solutions in a structured and informative manner that facilitates decision making. 
Your skills
You’re able to manage competing priorities, whilst keeping sight of the big picture and driving towards an end goal.
You are intellectually and technically curious and enjoy learning what things do and how they work.
You have a knack for understanding technical concepts and enjoy getting into the details with engineering and design. 
You have good communication skills that will be required to work with both internal and external stakeholders.
You’re comfortable handling ambiguity and working in a flat structure. 
You proactively take accountability for important and strategic activities.
Interview process
Interviewing is a two way process and we want you to have the time and opportunity to get to know us, as much as we are getting to know you! Our interviews are conversational and we want to get the best from you, so come with questions and be curious. In general you can expect the below, following a chat with one of our Talent Team:
45 minute with someone from the team
1 hour with two more people from the team including our Technical Product Director
45 minutes with two of the senior leadership team
33 days holiday (including public holidays, which you can take when it works best for you)
An extra day’s holiday for your birthday
Annual leave is increased with length of service, and you can choose to buy or sell up to five extra days off
16 hours paid volunteering time a year
Salary sacrifice, company enhanced pension scheme
Life insurance at 4x your salary & group income protection
Private Medical Insurance with VitalityHealth including mental health support and cancer care. Partner benefits include discounts with Waitrose, Mr&Mrs Smith and Peloton
Generous family-friendly policies
Incentives refer a friend scheme
Perkbox membership giving access to retail discounts, a wellness platform for physical and mental health, and weekly free and boosted perks
Access to initiatives like Cycle to Work, Salary Sacrificed Gym partnerships and Electric Vehicle (EV) leasing
You may be put off applying for a role because you don't tick every box. Forget that! While we can’t accommodate every flexible working request, we're always open to discussion. So, if you're excited about working with us, but aren’t sure if you're 100% there yet, get in touch anyway. We’re on a mission to radically reshape banking – and that starts with our brilliant team. Whatever came before, we’re proud to bring together people of all backgrounds and experiences who love working together to solve problems.
Engine by Starling is an equal opportunity employer, and we’re proud of our ongoing efforts to foster diversity & inclusion in the workplace. Individuals seeking employment at Engine by Starling are considered without regard to race, religion, national origin, age, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, medical condition, ancestry, physical or mental disability, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. 
When you provide us with this information, you are doing so at your own consent, with full knowledge that we will process this personal data in accordance with our Privacy Notice. By submitting your application, you agree that Engine by Starling and Starling will collect your personal data for recruiting and related purposes. Our Privacy Notice explains what personal information we will process, where we will process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over our use of your personal information.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Product Manager will take ownership of the Contact Centre product domain, overseeing the discovery, design, and delivery of new features. They will work cross-functionally to align product improvements with business vision and client needs.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have over 4 years of product management experience, ideally in FinTech, with a strong background in customer service and contact centre technology. They must demonstrate the ability to manage competing priorities and communicate effectively with stakeholders.
linkedin_org_locations: London Fruit and Wool Exchange, 1 Duval Square, London, London E1 6PW, GB
Brunel House, 2 Fitzalan Road, Cardiff, CF24 0HA, GB
Beresford House, Town Quay, Southampton, SO14 2AQ, GB
St Peter's Square, Landmark, Manchester, England M1 4PB, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Hello, we’re Starling.

Banking was broken – so we decided to fix it. The vision? Fast technology, fair service and honest values. All at the tap of a phone, all the time.

We built Britain’s first digital bank.

One hard-won banking licence later, we set about giving people a new way to spend, save and manage their money (and take better care of the planet, too).

We’re changing banking for good.

Back then, we were obsessed with unravelling the knotty world of finance and solving people’s problems rather than selling them stuff. We still are.

Since then, we’ve grown. A lot.

Over four million accounts (and four account types!). A team of thousands. Headquartered in London with offices in Cardiff, Dublin, Manchester and Southampton. Seven years voted Which? Recommended Provider and Britain's Best Banking Brand. Still zero branches.

Our culture is open, inclusive and focused on solving real customer problems, with an emphasis on doing the right thing, even when it’s not always the easy thing. From our approach to working together and sustainability to how we build our products, our decisions need to make the world – and Starling – a better place to be.

Everyone at Starling is essential to our mission, which is really quite simple: to solve our customer’s problems – and build the best bank in the world!

And now we're providing Starling to other banks, via a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) proposition through our subsidiary Engine by Starling, using the proprietary technology platform that it uses to power our own bank.

title: クリエイティブライタ
description_text:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.
At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence. 
What we do
The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.
About the Role
Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses — when projects are available. 
Responsibilities: 
Carefully review provided Japanese-language data (text, images, or videos)
Label and classify Japanese content in accordance with project guidelines
Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear elements in Japanese materials
This is project-based work, meaning tasks are available only when projects are active. The next project is expected to start in a few weeks. Depending on your profile and current opportunities, you may be invited to participate in one or more projects.
Bachelor’s degree in any discipline
Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role.
Proficient in Japanese (C2) and advanced in English (B2 or higher), both written and spoken.
Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities 
Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions 
Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed 
Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence
Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you? 
Get paid for your expertise, with rates that can go up to $4/hour depending on your skills, experience, location and project needs.
Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments.
Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio.
Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise.
ai_core_responsibilities: Annotators will review Japanese-language data, label and classify content according to project guidelines, and identify factually incorrect or inappropriate elements. This role involves participating in online projects related to AI content evaluation.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a bachelor's degree and at least one year of professional experience. Proficiency in Japanese (C2) and advanced English (B2 or higher) is required, along with strong attention to detail and communication skills.
linkedin_org_slogan: Be a key player in crafting the high-quality data essential for AI innovation. Perfect for aspiring freelancers
linkedin_org_description: Earn money today with the AI work of tomorrow. Toloka Annotators is the platform for the most curious and attentive annotators. Label projects from relevance scoring to detecting sarcasm and be at the heart of Gen AI.

title: Performance Marketing Specialist (Web)
description_text:
We are looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist to join our Web Subscription business. This role sits at the core of our growth engine and is responsible for scaling acquisition across Google Ads and Meta Ads, with a strong focus on profitability, experimentation, and long-term value.
This is a hands-on role for someone who has already operated at scale, is comfortable managing 8-figure monthly budgets, and understands how performance marketing works in subscription-based businesses.
You will work in a cross-channel environment (Search + Social), closely connected with Product, Data, Creative, and Lifecycle teams, and contribute directly to the growth of some of our most relevant Web products.
What you will do
Own and scale performance across Google Ads (Search, Performance Max, Discovery) and Meta Ads for Web subscription products
Manage and optimize 8-figure monthly budgets, ensuring efficiency, scalability, and profitability
Drive acquisition with a strong focus on LTV, CAC, payback and cohort performance, not just short-term CPA
Design and run experimentation roadmaps across channels, creatives, audiences, and bidding strategies
Collaborate closely with Creative teams to build and iterate high-impact creative testing loops
Work with Product and Lifecycle teams to improve onboarding, conversion, retention and renewal flows
Analyze performance at a granular level and translate insights into clear, actionable decisionsco
Contribute to the launch and scaling of new acquisition channels (e.g. TikTok, Reddit, new discovery formats) as part of a cross-channel strategy
Act as a senior contributor within the team, helping raise the bar in execution, thinking and best practices
How we think about this role
This is not a “SEM Specialist” or a “Paid Social Operator” role.
We are looking for someone who:
Thinks in systems, not channels
Optimizes for long-term growth, not short-term wins
Is comfortable owning complexity and making trade-offs at scale
Is used to working in very fast-paced, high-intensity environments, where priorities evolve quickly and execution speed matters
Why join us
Work on large-scale Web subscription products with real impact
Operate at a level of spend and complexity few teams reach
Be part of a modern Performance Marketing organization with a cross-channel mindset
High ownership, strong collaboration with Product, Data and Creative teams
Must-have
Strong hands-on experience managing Google Ads and Meta Ads at scale
Proven ownership of 8-figure monthly budgets in performance marketing environments
Deep understanding of performance fundamentals: bidding, auction dynamics, incrementality, attribution, experimentation
Data-driven mindset with the ability to interpret performance beyond surface metrics
Experience working in fast-paced, high-growth environments
Nice-to-have
Background in subscription-based businesses (Web or Apps)
Familiarity with LTV models, cohorts, churn, renewals and payback periods
Experience launching or scaling new paid channels beyond Google and Meta
Exposure to CRO, funnels and lifecycle marketing
Growth and career development
At Leadtech, we prioritize your growth. Enjoy a flexible career path with personalized internal training and an annual budget for external learning opportunities.
Work-Life balance
Benefit from a flexible schedule with flextime (7 - 9:30 a.m. start, 3:30 - 6 p.m. end) and the option of working full remote or from our Barcelona office. Enjoy free Friday afternoons with a 7-hour workday, plus a 35-hour workweek in July and August so you can savor summer!
Comprehensive benefits
Competitive salary, full-time permanent contract, and top-tier private health insurance (including dental and psychological services).
25 days of vacation plus your birthday off, with flexible vacation options—no blackout days!
Unique Perks
If you wish to come, in our office in Barcelona you’ll find it complete with free coffee, fresh fruit, snacks, a game room, and a rooftop terrace with stunning Mediterranean views.
Additional benefits include ticket restaurant and nursery vouchers, paid directly from your gross salary.
Join us in an environment where you’re free to innovate, learn, and grow alongside passionate professionals. At Leadtech, you’ll tackle exciting challenges and be part of a vibrant team dedicated to delivering exceptional user experiences
Sounds good? Apply now! We’re looking forward to getting to know you.
Equal Employment Opportunity Employer:
Leadtech is an Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Employer, which means we encourage applications from people with different backgrounds, interests, and personal circumstances. Our team welcomes applicants regardless of their race, gender, age, religion, nationality, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expressión and/or disabilities. All we need is your high energy, skills, and willingness to be a part of a great project!
Location
You'll have the flexibility to choose whether you'd like to come to the office every day, from time to time, or work fully remote. We want you to find the best combination for you.
If you prefer to be surrounded with amazing people, our exceptional office is in Barcelona’s Blue Building, located right on the city's seafront. Besides our stunning views, you’ll enjoy our office perks such as free fruit, snacks, and coffee and you’ll also be able to take part in our Mario Kart and table tennis competitions.
The personal data you provide will be processed in order to manage your candidacy for the corporate selection processes that fit your profile. If you wish, you can exercise your rights of access, rectification or cancellation by writing to our address (Avenida Litoral, 12-14, 5ta planta. Barcelona. 08005) or to the email address protecciondedatos@LeadTech.com, attaching to your request a document that can validate your identity.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Performance Marketing Specialist will own and scale performance across Google Ads and Meta Ads for Web subscription products, managing 8-figure monthly budgets. They will drive acquisition with a focus on long-term value and collaborate with various teams to enhance product performance.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have strong hands-on experience managing Google Ads and Meta Ads at scale, along with a deep understanding of performance marketing fundamentals. Experience in fast-paced, high-growth environments and familiarity with subscription-based businesses are preferred.
linkedin_org_locations: Av. del Litoral, 12, Barcelona, Barcelona 08005, ES
linkedin_org_description:
At Leadtech, we work hard... and play harder! Our mission is to bring forward new business ideas and empower employees to achieve their goals in the online business world.

Since 2009, we have been fostering innovative and creative techniques across many industries, making us pioneers in online project management.

Leadtech is dedicated to constant improvement and inspiring new ideas daily for the world we live in and the future to come.

We think big... and work bigger, and that's why we do business internationally with over 600 passionate professionals who speak over 15 languages fluently. A global team of focused experts in a range of fields:

We analyze, we socialize, we write, we code, we design, we calculate, we decide, we collaborate, we generate, we engage, we program, we create… The real question is what don’t we do?!

With a truly modern approach to company culture, our values, diversity, flexibility and an active work-play balance are what make Leadtech unique.

title: Technical Writer
locations_alt_raw: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
locations_derived: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
description_text:
USFS is an ISO 9001 management consulting firm with headquarters in the National Capital Region and offices and staff in 12 US states. USFS staff average 10+ years of professional experience, over 80 percent of our staff have bachelor’s degrees, and over half have advanced degrees in business and technology. USFS was founded in 2010 and provides financial management, acquisition management, program management, and data analytic support services to federal and state governments. US Federal Solutions, a leading management consulting firm, is seeking Acquisition Professionals to support the US Coast Guard.
Technical Writer / Documentation SME (Federal Acquisition | Section 508)
Company: US Federal Solutions (USFS)
Client: U.S. Coast Guard
Location: Remote
Schedule: Full-time | Monday–Friday (must support government core hours)
Work Authorization: U.S. Citizen required
Security/Access: Must be able to obtain Public Trust and be eligible for CAC issuance
About the role
US Federal Solutions is seeking a Technical Writer / Documentation Subject Matter Expert (SME) to support the U.S. Coast Guard. This role is dedicated exclusively to PWS Task Area 2.3 and is responsible for producing, standardizing, and improving a high volume of acquisition and operational documentation.
You’ll be the person who ensures Coast Guard documentation is clear, consistent, compliant, and ready for leadership review or audit at any time. If you’ve built documentation programs in DHS or DoD environments and you take pride in clean, compliant deliverables, this is a strong fit.
What you’ll do
Own the end-to-end production and standardization of 300+ deliverables annually, ensuring quality, consistency, and on-time delivery 
Develop and refine acquisition and program documentation (templates, guides, SOPs, reports, briefings, and other formal deliverables) 
Ensure documents meet Section 508 compliance requirements and are accessible, properly formatted, and publication-ready 
Establish and enforce documentation standards (style guides, templates, version control, naming conventions, review workflows) 
Edit and quality-check documents for clarity, accuracy, completeness, and audit readiness 
Coordinate inputs and reviews across multiple stakeholders to drive timely approvals and reduce rework 
Maintain document libraries and support continuous improvement of documentation processes
Why US Federal Solutions
At USFS, you’ll support a mission that matters and work with teams that value quality, accountability, and practical execution. This role gives you ownership of documentation quality and standards across a large and visible body of work.
Required qualifications
Bachelors Degree
Proven technical writing and documentation leadership experience in DHS and/or DoD environments 
Strong experience developing acquisition-related documents (e.g., SOW/PWS support content, SOPs, program documentation, formal correspondence, reporting) 
Demonstrated expertise in Section 508 compliance and accessible document production 
Ability to manage high-volume deliverable production while maintaining quality and consistency 
Strong editing skills and attention to detail (grammar, structure, formatting, and document control) 
Proficiency with Microsoft 365 (Word, PowerPoint, Excel) and producing polished PDFs 
Ability to work independently, manage competing priorities, and meet deadlines in a government environment 
Preferred qualifications
Experience building or managing documentation standards programs (templates, style guides, QA checklists) 
Familiarity with procurement-sensitive content handling and audit-readiness expectations 
Experience supporting technical or operational organizations with multiple stakeholders and fast turnaround times
Security and compliance requirements
U.S. Citizenship required 
Ability to obtain/maintain Public Trust 
Eligible for CAC issuance 
Ability to handle sensitive/procurement-related information appropriately and comply with DHS/USCG information protection requirements
Employer-provided paid Medical / Dental / Vision insurance.
Employer matching 401K plan.
PTO
11 Federal Holidays
Additionally voluntary benefits to include Short / Long Term Disability, Term Life, and AD&D Insurance.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Technical Writer will produce, standardize, and improve a high volume of acquisition and operational documentation for the U.S. Coast Guard. This includes ensuring documentation is clear, consistent, compliant, and ready for leadership review or audit.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a bachelor's degree and proven technical writing experience in DHS and/or DoD environments. Strong editing skills, attention to detail, and proficiency with Microsoft 365 are also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: U.S. Federal Solutions is a management and IT consulting firm providing proven methods for improving performance.
linkedin_org_locations: 8710 Cameron Street, Suite 907, Silver Spring, MD 20910, US
linkedin_org_description: U.S. Federal Solutions, Inc. is a management and IT consulting firm founded in 2010.  We provide support to federal and state agencies in strategic planning, data analytics, performance management, and IT consulting services.  Our consultants’ average more than 15 years of professional experience and our staff have advanced degrees in business or technology.

title: Software Quality Assurance Intern
description_text:
As a QA Intern at testRigor, you will have the hands-on opportunity to work with a growing team to solve exciting challenges. Our team is agile, highly collaborative, and learning-focused.
Prospective interns should be highly-motivated self-starters who can drive ideas independently and collaborate effectively in a team setting.
We are also open to offering the full-time position post Internship period, subject to the Intern's performance.
Stipend: $500 per month
Duration of the Internship: 12 Months
Responsibilities:
● Participate in requirements review and understand new feature development 
● Create automated test cases for testRigor’s customers or prospects using the provided documentation
● Collaborate with the software engineering team on QA/Testing topics 
● Gather requirements, build testing solutions and troubleshoot issues to deliver a high-quality product. 
● Communicate technical concepts/issues clearly to internal stakeholders
● Develop, document, and maintain test cases and validation reports
● Report issues through a formal bug tracking system and work with the engineering team to help isolate, debug, and resolve issues/bugs
Qualification:
● Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related field in progress. Last year students are welcome to apply!
● Comfortable working in a dynamic, fast-paced, changing environment
● Strong customer focus, excellent problem solving, and analytical skills
● Ability to work until the issue is resolved
● Fluency in the English Language is a must!
● This position may require working during U.S. hours.
Please, share your MBTI type in your cover letter.
(If you don't know your type, here is a free test online: https://personalityjunkie.com/free-online-personality-test-3/ )
ai_core_responsibilities: Interns will participate in requirements review and create automated test cases. They will collaborate with the software engineering team and troubleshoot issues to ensure high-quality product delivery.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should be pursuing a Bachelor's degree in a related field and possess strong problem-solving and analytical skills. Fluency in English and the ability to work in a dynamic environment are also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: The #1 Generative AI-based test automation tool. Create and execute QA tests in plain English.
linkedin_org_locations: 149 New Montgomery St, San Francisco, California 94105, US
linkedin_org_description:
testRigor is a Generative AI-based codeless test automation tool helping companies empower manual testers to build test automation via executable specifications in plain English 15X faster than with Selenium and spend 200X less time maintaining tests compare to Selenium.

testRigor is best applied as a nocode automation testing tool for cross-system end-to-end testing to build tests spanning web, mobile, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Microsoft Dynamics, SAP, and any other 3rd party systems in one simple test.

Because tests in testRigor are written in plain English from end-user's perspective, they withstand changes in implementation details, allow fast test development, and dramatically reduce test maintenance efforts.

On top of that, because all the tests are in plain English, it significantly improves collaboration within your company, allowing product managers to take part in assessing test maintenance and making sure that the product works as intended.

The vast majority of our customers achieve 100% test automation coverage within under 12 months of deployment.

testRigor is available as SaaS and on-premise versions.

#testRigor #generativeai #testautomation #generativeaiinqa #genaiinqa #genai #aiinsoftwaretesting #qa #testing

title: Freelance Energy Engineer with Python Experience - AI Trainer
description_text:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency. 
Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.
What this opportunity involves 
While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may: 
Design rigorous energy engineering problems reflecting professional practice; 
Evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints; 
Validate calculations or simulations using Python (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy); 
Improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic; 
Apply structured scoring criteria to multi-step problems.
What we look for 
This opportunity is a good fit for energy engineers with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have: 
Degree in Energy Engineering or related fields, e.g. Electrical Engineering, Power Systems Engineering, Renewable Energy Engineering, Electronics etc. 
3+ years of professional energy engineering experience 
Strong written English (C1/C2) 
Strong Python proficiency for numerical validation 
Stable internet connection 
Professional certifications (e.g., PE, CEng, EMP, CEM) and experience in international or applied projects are an advantage.
How it works 
Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid
Project time expectations 
For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10–20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active. 
Payment 
Paid contributions, with rates up to $40/hour* 
Fixed project rate or individual rates, depending on the project 
Some projects include incentive payments 
*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.
ai_core_responsibilities: Contributors will design energy engineering problems and evaluate AI solutions for correctness. They will also validate calculations or simulations using Python and improve AI reasoning.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a degree in Energy Engineering or related fields and at least 3 years of professional experience. Strong proficiency in Python and written English is required, along with a stable internet connection.
linkedin_org_slogan: Be the “I” in AI with Mindrift. Work from anywhere on groundbreaking AI projects
linkedin_org_description:
Welcome to Mindrift — a space where innovation meets opportunity. We’re a pioneering platform dedicated to advancing the field of artificial intelligence through collaborative online projects. Our focus lies in creating data for generative AI, offering a unique chance for freelancers to contribute to AI development from anywhere, at any time.

At Mindrift, we believe in the power of collective intelligence to shape the future of AI. Our platform allows users to dive into a variety of tasks — ranging from creating training prompts for AI models to refining AI responses for greater relevance.

Let’s build the future of AI together, one task at a time.

*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.
ai_core_responsibilities: Contributors will design rigorous energy engineering problems, evaluate AI solutions for correctness against industry constraints, and validate calculations or simulations using Python libraries.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be energy engineers with at least 3 years of professional experience and strong proficiency in Python for numerical validation, alongside strong written English skills (C1/C2).
linkedin_org_slogan: Be the “I” in AI with Mindrift. Work from anywhere on groundbreaking AI projects
linkedin_org_description:
Welcome to Mindrift — a space where innovation meets opportunity. We’re a pioneering platform dedicated to advancing the field of artificial intelligence through collaborative online projects. Our focus lies in creating data for generative AI, offering a unique chance for freelancers to contribute to AI development from anywhere, at any time.

At Mindrift, we believe in the power of collective intelligence to shape the future of AI. Our platform allows users to dive into a variety of tasks — ranging from creating training prompts for AI models to refining AI responses for greater relevance.

Let’s build the future of AI together, one task at a time.

title: CNC Applications Engineer
description_text:
As a key architect of the Phillips Value Chain, you aren't just an engineer; you are a catalyst for industrial transformation. We are seeking a relentless life-long learner and technical artist who views CNC machining and CAM programming as a master tradecraft. You will take radical ownership of our customers' most complex challenges, immersing yourself in the latest cutting-edge software, work-holding innovations, and tooling technologies. By mastering everything from 5-axis simultaneous milling to complex mill-turn applications, you will become the indispensable link that bridges the gap between raw hardware and peak manufacturing performance.
Your mission is to dismantle the status quo and set the global benchmark for technical education and application. You will revolutionize our curriculum, transforming Phillip’s training courses into a world-class experience that delivers legendary value and compels customers to return time and time again. Whether you are performing high-stakes machine simulations, stock verifications, or onsite post-processor integrations, your work will set the bar by which all other resellers are measured. You will provide expert-level diagnostic support to our service teams and deliver breathtaking live demonstrations that prove the untapped profit potential of 5-axis and mill: turn technology, effectively turning every machine setup into a masterclass of efficiency.
Beyond the machine tool, you are a strategic partner to our sales force and a mentor to our clients. You will arm our sales teams with the technical firepower they need—from lightning-fast time studies to articulating the high-level ROI of CAD/CAM software—to ensure customers understand the "Phillips Advantage." You’ll be a laser-focused problem solver who thrives on the road, traveling to customer sites to lead run-offs and overcome any obstacle in your path. By expanding our scope of work through relentless innovation and building a reputation as the industry’s most sought-after resource, you won't just support the market—you will lead it.
Phillips offers a world-beating collaborative culture and competitive pay and benefits. In our 65 year history, Phillips has grown from a handful of employees to over 900 supplying and supporting manufacturing technology in the United States and around the world. Compensation at Phillips is based on the market and your individual level of contribution. The expected pay range for a qualified person in this role is between $90,000.00 and $120,000.00 annually plus bonus opportunities. If you have a different suggestion, please mention it in your application.
Phillips Corporation is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected Veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law. Phillips Corporation is an E-Verify participant. 
Expert-level proficiency in CAD modeling and CAM programming, including complex multi-axis workflows and digital manufacturing integration
High-level experience in Haas controls and Mastercam 5-axis or Mill: Turn machining strategies, simulation, and optimization
Ability to configure, test, validate, and deploy post processors across multiple machine platforms
Expertise in machine simulation, stock verification, probing systems, and virtual commissioning environments
Advanced use of Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and data tools for technical documentation, training content, time studies, and executive-level presentations
Ability to travel 75% of the time or more nationally
Due to customer requirements, candidates must be a US citizen to be considered
Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental & Vision)
Retirement Plan (401k, IRA)
Life Insurance (Basic, Voluntary & AD&D)
Paid Time Off (Vacation, Sick & Public Holidays)
Car and cell phone allowance
Family Leave (Maternity, Paternity)
Short Term & Long Term Disability
Training & Development
Work From Home
ai_core_responsibilities: You will take ownership of complex customer challenges and provide expert-level support, including high-stakes machine simulations and live demonstrations. Your mission is to revolutionize training courses and ensure customers understand the value of Phillips' offerings.
ai_requirements_summary: Expert-level proficiency in CAD modeling and CAM programming is required, along with high-level experience in Haas controls and Mastercam. Candidates must be U.S. citizens and able to travel 75% of the time or more.
linkedin_org_slogan: Experience Manufacturing Innovation
linkedin_org_specialties: and Haas Maintenance and Repair Training
linkedin_org_locations: 7390 Coca Cola Dr, Hanover, MD 21076, US
linkedin_org_description:
For more than 60 years, federal government, industry and education leaders have trusted the people at Phillips Corporation to solve their greatest manufacturing challenges.   With a worldwide presence and partnerships with premier technology leaders like Haas Automation and EOS, our goal is to create legendary value for the manufacturing community by unlocking solutions to propel manufacturing capabilities, profitability, and productivity. 

Phillips is a global manufacturing solutions leader that started as a machine tool distributor, we’ve most recently established a Public-Private Partnership agreement to work with the U.S. Department of Defense to educate, inspire, and redefine the capabilities of additive manufacturing. 

Experience Manufacturing Innovation with Phillips! 

Connect with us and  learn more about the pride we take in the people who join us:  www.phillipscorp.com/showcase/careers 

Phillips Corporation Divisions:

Phillips Commercial Division (Colfax, NC, Philadelphia, PA) 
Phillips Federal Division (US)  www,phillipscorp.com/federal
Phillips Machine Tools India (India) www.phillipscorp.com/india

title: 3927-Software Development Engineer-I Backend (Prior Auth)
description_text:
Engineering at Innovaccer
With every line of code, we accelerate our customers' success, turning complex challenges into innovative solutions. Collaboratively, we transform each data point we gather into valuable insights for our customers. Join us and be part of a team that's turning dreams of better healthcare into reality, one line of code at a time. Together, we’re shaping the future and making a meaningful impact on the world.
Your Role
We at Innovaccer are looking for a Software Development Engineer-I (Backend) to build the most amazing product experience. You’ll get to work with other engineers to build delightful feature experiences to understand and solve our customer’s pain points.
A Day in the Life
● Design, develop, and maintain robust and scalable backend systems using Python, FastAPI, Applied AI, Backend engineering, MongoDB, Redis, Kafka, CI/CD, etc.
● Collaborate closely with product managers, frontend engineers, and other stakeholders to understand requirements, define technical solutions, and deliver high-quality software products.
● Develop efficient and optimized APIs and microservices to support various frontend applications and external integrations.
● Implement data models, and database schemas.
● Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code following industry best practices and coding standards. 
●Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues, ensuring system reliability, stability, and security
What You Need
● Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent work experience). 
●1-3 years of professional experience in backend development, with a focus on Python, FastAPI, Applied AI, Backend engineering, MongoDB, Redis, Kafka, CI/CD, etc.
● Proficiency in microservices, application Design, TDD, develop maintainable and efficient backend systems, CI/CD, observability.
● Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to debug and resolve complex issues.
Here’s What We Offer
Generous Leaves: Enjoy generous leave benefits of up to 40 days.
Parental Leave: Leverage one of industry's best parental leave policies to spend time with your new addition.
Sabbatical: Want to focus on skill development, pursue an academic career, or just take a break? We've got you covered.
Health Insurance: We offer comprehensive health insurance to support you and your family, covering medical expenses related to illness, disease, or injury. Extending support to the family members who matter most.
Care Program: Whether it’s a celebration or a time of need, we’ve got you covered with care vouchers to mark major life events. Through our Care Vouchers program, employees receive thoughtful gestures for significant personal milestones and moments of need.
Financial Assistance: Life happens, and when it does, we’re here to help. Our financial assistance policy offers support through salary advances and personal loans for genuine personal needs, ensuring help is there when you need it most.

Innovaccer is an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity, and we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace where all employees, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, marital status, or veteran status, feel valued and empowered.

Disclaimer: Innovaccer does not charge fees or require payment from individuals or agencies for securing employment with us. We do not guarantee job spots or engage in any financial transactions related to employment. If you encounter any posts or requests asking for payment or personal information, we strongly advise you to report them immediately to our HR department at px@innovaccer.com. Additionally, please exercise caution and verify the authenticity of any requests before disclosing personal and confidential information, including bank account details.

About Innovaccer
Innovaccer Inc. is the data platform that accelerates innovation. The Innovaccer platform unifies patient data across systems and care settings and empowers healthcare organizations with scalable, modern applications that improve clinical, financial, operational, and experiential outcomes. Innovaccer’s EHR-agnostic solutions have been deployed across more than 1,600 hospitals and clinics in the US, enabling care delivery transformation for more than 96,000 clinicians, and helping providers work collaboratively with payers and life sciences companies. Innovaccer has helped its customers unify health records for more than 54 million people and generate over $1.5 billion in cumulative cost savings. The Innovaccer platform is the #1 rated Best-in-KLAS data and analytics platform by KLAS, and the #1 rated population health technology platform by Black Book. For more information, please visit innovaccer.com.Check us out on YouTube, Glassdoor, LinkedIn, and innovaccer.com.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves designing, developing, and maintaining robust backend systems while collaborating with product managers and frontend engineers. The engineer will also troubleshoot complex technical issues to ensure system reliability and security.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a bachelor's degree in a related field and 1-3 years of professional experience in backend development, particularly with Python and related technologies. Strong problem-solving skills and proficiency in microservices and application design are essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: The AI Cloud for Healthcare Performance
linkedin_org_locations: 101 Mission Street, Suite 1950, San Francisco, CA 94105, San Francisco, California 94105, US
linkedin_org_description: Innovaccer activates the flow of healthcare data, empowering providers, payers, and government organizations to deliver intelligent and connected experiences that advance health outcomes. The Healthcare Intelligence Cloud equips every stakeholder in the patient journey to turn fragmented data into proactive, coordinated actions that elevate the quality of care and drive operational performance. Leading healthcare organizations like CommonSpirit Health, Atlantic Health, and Banner Health trust Innovaccer to integrate a system of intelligence into their existing infrastructure— extending the human touch in healthcare. For more information, visit www.innovaccer.com.

title: CNC Applications Engineer
description_text:
As a key architect of the Phillips Value Chain, you aren't just an engineer; you are a catalyst for industrial transformation. We are seeking a relentless life-long learner and technical artist who views CNC machining and CAM programming as a master tradecraft. You will take radical ownership of our customers' most complex challenges, immersing yourself in the latest cutting-edge software, work-holding innovations, and tooling technologies. By mastering everything from 5-axis simultaneous milling to complex mill-turn applications, you will become the indispensable link that bridges the gap between raw hardware and peak manufacturing performance.
Your mission is to dismantle the status quo and set the global benchmark for technical education and application. You will revolutionize our curriculum, transforming Phillip’s training courses into a world-class experience that delivers legendary value and compels customers to return time and time again. Whether you are performing high-stakes machine simulations, stock verifications, or onsite post-processor integrations, your work will set the bar by which all other resellers are measured. You will provide expert-level diagnostic support to our service teams and deliver breathtaking live demonstrations that prove the untapped profit potential of 5-axis and mill: turn technology, effectively turning every machine setup into a masterclass of efficiency.
Beyond the machine tool, you are a strategic partner to our sales force and a mentor to our clients. You will arm our sales teams with the technical firepower they need—from lightning-fast time studies to articulating the high-level ROI of CAD/CAM software—to ensure customers understand the "Phillips Advantage." You’ll be a laser-focused problem solver who thrives on the road, traveling to customer sites to lead run-offs and overcome any obstacle in your path. By expanding our scope of work through relentless innovation and building a reputation as the industry’s most sought-after resource, you won't just support the market—you will lead it.
Phillips offers a world-beating collaborative culture and competitive pay and benefits. In our 65 year history, Phillips has grown from a handful of employees to over 900 supplying and supporting manufacturing technology in the United States and around the world. Compensation at Phillips is based on the market and your individual level of contribution. The expected pay range for a qualified person in this role is between $90,000.00 and $120,000.00 annually plus bonus opportunities. If you have a different suggestion, please mention it in your application.
Phillips Corporation is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected Veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law. Phillips Corporation is an E-Verify participant. 
Expert-level proficiency in CAD modeling and CAM programming, including complex multi-axis workflows and digital manufacturing integration
High-level experience in Haas controls and Mastercam 5-axis or Mill: Turn machining strategies, simulation, and optimization
Ability to configure, test, validate, and deploy post processors across multiple machine platforms
Expertise in machine simulation, stock verification, probing systems, and virtual commissioning environments
Advanced use of Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and data tools for technical documentation, training content, time studies, and executive-level presentations
Ability to travel 75% of the time or more nationally
Due to customer requirements, candidates must be a US citizen to be considered
Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental & Vision)
Retirement Plan (401k, IRA)
Life Insurance (Basic, Voluntary & AD&D)
Paid Time Off (Vacation, Sick & Public Holidays)
Car and cell phone allowance
Family Leave (Maternity, Paternity)
Short Term & Long Term Disability
Training & Development
Work From Home
ai_core_responsibilities: You will take ownership of complex customer challenges and provide expert-level support, including high-stakes machine simulations and live demonstrations. Your mission is to revolutionize training courses and ensure customers understand the value of Phillips' offerings.
ai_requirements_summary: Expert-level proficiency in CAD modeling and CAM programming is required, along with high-level experience in Haas controls and Mastercam. Candidates must be U.S. citizens and able to travel 75% of the time or more.
linkedin_org_slogan: Experience Manufacturing Innovation
linkedin_org_specialties: and Haas Maintenance and Repair Training
linkedin_org_locations: 7390 Coca Cola Dr, Hanover, MD 21076, US
linkedin_org_description:
For more than 60 years, federal government, industry and education leaders have trusted the people at Phillips Corporation to solve their greatest manufacturing challenges.   With a worldwide presence and partnerships with premier technology leaders like Haas Automation and EOS, our goal is to create legendary value for the manufacturing community by unlocking solutions to propel manufacturing capabilities, profitability, and productivity. 

Phillips is a global manufacturing solutions leader that started as a machine tool distributor, we’ve most recently established a Public-Private Partnership agreement to work with the U.S. Department of Defense to educate, inspire, and redefine the capabilities of additive manufacturing. 

Experience Manufacturing Innovation with Phillips! 

Connect with us and  learn more about the pride we take in the people who join us:  www.phillipscorp.com/showcase/careers

title: IT Officer
locations_alt_raw: San José, San José Province, Costa Rica
description_text:
Duty Station: San José, Costa Rica
Type of employment: Part-time
Duration of contract: 12 months with possible extension
Work model: Hybrid – combines work in the office and remote work
Line Manager: Head of Regional Office
Monthly Gross Salary: 736 USD
In People in Need, we believe that people everywhere in the world should have the right to make decisions about their lives and to share the rights expressed in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights.
PIN is one of the key organizations from the Central European region supporting human rights defenders and independent media within our Centre for Human Rights and Democracy (HRD). The HRD’s primary goal is to support independent individuals and groups in repressive and fragile environments who are the target of persecution, harassment, or arrests because of their opinions or activities, and to strengthen civil society in countries that are going through democratic transformation.
Although our Latin American (LATAM) program is located in San Jose, Costa Rica, we all belong to the larger HRD team, united by a shared vision and common goals.
As IT Officer you will be responsible for providing IT support to the Centre of Human Rights Department in Latin America, based in Costa Rica. You will ensure the timely resolution of IT-related issues, maintenance of office equipment and systems set up. You will work in close collaboration with the Headquarters IT department, programme teams, and other operational staff to develop coordinated approaches and joint solutions.
Your duties and responsibilities:
Manage day-to-day IT operations, including installation, configuration, maintenance, and backup of software, computers, and office equipment.
Provide technical support and maintenance for IT, office, and communication systems.
Maintain an up-to-date inventory of IT assets, licenses, and service materials.
Assist Procurement Officer in IT-related procurements, including identifying suppliers and liaising with local vendors such as communication service providers, electricians, and other IT-related suppliers.
Train and support staff in the effective and secure use of technologies and digital tools.
Requirements:
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Computer Engineering or related field.
1-2 years of relevant professional experience.
Hands-on experience troubleshooting end-user issues (hardware, software, connectivity and related).
Proven experience in network, systems, MS Office, and web-based information systems.
Knowledge of Android and iOS platforms.
Excellent interpersonal skills and ability to work in a multicultural environment.
Ability to manage stress and maintain professionalism in challenging situations.
Demonstrates commitment to humanitarian values and confidentiality.
Fluent Spanish and English proficiency at B2 level or above.
Applicants must be legally authorized to work in Costa Rica (citizenship or valid work permit).
We offer:
Well - established organization: Possibility to work in an NGO with an international outreach.
Meaning: The meaningful job in a team of experienced and highly motivated people.
Flexibility: Flexible working hours and the possibility to work partly from home (hybrid work model)
Time Off: 25 days of paid leave per year and 3 sick leave days.
Remuneration: monthly salary of 736 USD, Severance clearance yearly.
Contract: 12 months with possible extension.
Interested candidates are encouraged to send their CV and cover letter (up to 1 page) in English.
ai_core_responsibilities: As an IT Officer, you will manage day-to-day IT operations and provide technical support for IT and communication systems. You will also maintain an inventory of IT assets and assist in IT-related procurements.
ai_requirements_summary: A bachelor's degree in a relevant field and 1-2 years of professional experience are required. Candidates should have hands-on experience troubleshooting end-user issues and be fluent in Spanish and English.
linkedin_org_slogan: Help with us
linkedin_org_description:
We are a non-governmental, non-profit organisation based on the ideas of humanism, freedom, equality and solidarity. Since our founding in 1992, we have developed and established ourselves as one of the largest non-profit organizations in Central and Eastern Europe. We are respected above all else for our ability to respond quickly and efficiently. 

We provide humanitarian aid, fight poverty and malnutrition, strengthen civil society and engage in international development cooperation. We also work tirelessly to stand up for human rights around the world. Finally, in the Czech Republic, we focus on issues of education and social inclusion. Today, we work in 42 different countries around the world.

For more information, please visit: https://www.peopleinneed.net/

---------------------------------------------------------
Jsme nevládní nezisková organizace vycházející z myšlenek humanismu, svobody, rovnosti a solidarity. Od doby svého vzniku v roce 1992 jsme se rozvinuli a etablovali jako jedna z největších neziskových organizací ve střední a východní Evropě. Jsme respektováni především pro svou schopnost flexibilně reagovat a efektivně přinášet výsledky.
Poskytujeme humanitární pomoc v krizových oblastech, bojujeme s chudobou a podvýživou, posilujeme občanskou společnost a věnujeme se rozvojové spolupráci. Dále podporujeme dodržování lidských práv ve světě a v neposlední řadě se zaměřujeme také na oblasti vzdělávání v České republice a pomoci spoluobčanům žijícím v sociálním vyloučení. Dnes pracujeme ve 42 zemích světa.

Pro více informací navštivte www.clovekvtisni.cz

title: Freelance Energy Engineer with Python Experience - AI Trainer
description_text:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency. 
Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.
What this opportunity involves 
While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may: 
Design rigorous energy engineering problems reflecting professional practice; 
Evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints; 
Validate calculations or simulations using Python (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy); 
Improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic; 
Apply structured scoring criteria to multi-step problems.
What we look for 
This opportunity is a good fit for energy engineers with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have: 
Degree in Energy Engineering or related fields, e.g. Electrical Engineering, Power Systems Engineering, Renewable Energy Engineering, Electronics etc. 
3+ years of professional energy engineering experience 
Strong written English (C1/C2) 
Strong Python proficiency for numerical validation 
Stable internet connection 
Professional certifications (e.g., PE, CEng, EMP, CEM) and experience in international or applied projects are an advantage.
How it works 
Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid
Project time expectations 
For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10–20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active. 
Payment 
Paid contributions, with rates up to $40/hour* 
Fixed project rate or individual rates, depending on the project 
Some projects include incentive payments 
*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.
ai_core_responsibilities: Contributors will design rigorous energy engineering problems reflecting professional practice and evaluate AI solutions for correctness, assumptions, and constraints. They will also validate calculations using Python libraries and improve AI reasoning to align with industry-standard logic.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be energy engineers with at least 3 years of professional experience and strong proficiency in Python for numerical validation, holding a degree in Energy Engineering or related fields. Strong written English (C1/C2) is mandatory for this project-based role.
linkedin_org_slogan: Be the “I” in AI with Mindrift. Work from anywhere on groundbreaking AI projects
linkedin_org_description:
Welcome to Mindrift — a space where innovation meets opportunity. We’re a pioneering platform dedicated to advancing the field of artificial intelligence through collaborative online projects. Our focus lies in creating data for generative AI, offering a unique chance for freelancers to contribute to AI development from anywhere, at any time.

At Mindrift, we believe in the power of collective intelligence to shape the future of AI. Our platform allows users to dive into a variety of tasks — ranging from creating training prompts for AI models to refining AI responses for greater relevance.

Let’s build the future of AI together, one task at a time.

title: HGV Class 2 Driver (Morning Shifts)
description_text:
Job Opportunity: HGV / CAT C Drivers for Morning and Night Shifts in North West London
Are you a professional Class 2 driver looking for a rewarding role which is making a real difference? We are currently looking for a dedicated HGV Class 2 driver to join our driving team. The role comes with an extensive benefits package, including a competitive salary, a monthly welfare allowance, performance-related quarterly bonuses and more! You will also have the opportunity to be working for London's only BCORP certified waste management company.
First Mile is London's premier recycling service, collecting and recycling a range of waste from over 30,000 customers across the city. We are looking for a hard working driver with a passion for excellent customer service and creating a force for good. If this sounds like you, then this role is perfect!
Responsibilities:
- Starting and finishing your shift from our Operations Depot in North Acton 
- Driving our Class 2 vehicles around the city safely and professionally
- Using our innovative app to follow the most efficient routes and mark off customer collections, making your round as smooth and easy to complete as possible
- Collecting refuse bags and bins of various weights from clients.
- Effectively communicating any issues on your round in a timely manner
- Returning to the depot at the end of your shift.
Shift Pattern
- This role operates on a 4 days on, 2 days off continuous shift pattern. 
- Rounds typically start between 5am and 8am depending on the round you are assigned
- Each shift is 10 hours of work, plus a 45 minute unpaid break 
- Overtime is available and is paid at your standard hourly rate based on your salary
- A valid HGV UK driving license.
- A standard UK driving license with no more than 6 points
- Experience driving in a city, preferably London.
- The right to work in the UK (British Passport or visa valid for at least 12 months from start date).
- Good English language skills
- Happy with carrying out a physical role which requires heavy lifting and time on feet 
- A passion for delivering excellent customer service and a strong work ethic
Joining First Mile comes with a range of benefits, including:
- Competitive Salary 
- Annual Leave: 20 days (plus bank holidays) plus 1 extra day for each complete year of service up to 5 days.
- Quarterly Bonuses: Rewarding outstanding performance.
- Driver of the Quarter Bonus: Additional prizes for top-performing drivers.
- Monthly Allowance: £40 to spend as you wish – gym membership, treats, travel, it's up to you!
- Health and Financial Services: Access to free online GP and financial advice services.
- Family Leave: Enhanced policies to support your family needs.
If you have a great attitude, excellent driving skills, and a passion for customer service, we want to hear from you. Join First Mile and be a part of a sector that's more important than ever!
---
"HGV Driver," "CAT C Driver," "morning shift driver jobs London," "recycling service driver," "class 2 driver"
ai_key_skills: Right to Work in the UK
ai_core_responsibilities: The driver will start and finish shifts from the North Acton depot, driving Class 2 vehicles safely around the city while using an app to follow efficient routes and complete customer collections. Responsibilities also include collecting refuse bags and bins of various weights and communicating any issues encountered during the round.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a valid HGV UK driving license with no more than 6 points on their standard UK license and must have the right to work in the UK for at least 12 months. The role requires experience driving in a city, good English skills, and the ability to perform a physical role involving heavy lifting.
linkedin_org_slogan:
Helping over 30,000 UK businesses reduce their carbon impact with our range of recycling services.

B Corp Certified.
linkedin_org_locations: 70-71 Wells Street, London, England W1T 3QE, GB
linkedin_org_description:
First Mile are one of London's greenest recycling & waste management companies, helping over 30,000 businesses reduce their carbon impact with a range of recycling & commercial waste services.

We’re the first waste management company in London to become B Corp Certified.

Our tailored approach to recycling & waste management offers reliable & flexible rubbish collection, waste removal, & recycling services at competitive prices with 24/7 support. Our rubbish removal & waste management services range from zero-to-landfill general waste & recycling to specialist recycling services such as confidential waste & secure shredding.

Join over 30,000 London businesses reducing their carbon impact with our reliable waste collections, 24/7 support & a range of rubbish recycling services.

title: Client Coordinator
description_text:
Client Coordinator
Eight Financial Advice - Whiteley
£27,000

Do you love building relationships, creating opportunities, and delivering outstanding client experiences?
As a Client Coordinator, you’ll play a key part in keeping our clients connected and engaged while also supporting the growth of new business. You’ll ensure clients receive the highest level of service, from regular contact and reviews through to first impressions for new prospects. Alongside this, you’ll help generate new opportunities, book adviser appointments, and work with colleagues across the business to deliver meaningful results.
Key Responsibilities:
Build and maintain strong relationships with existing clients, ensuring they feel supported and valued
Book client and prospect appointments into adviser diaries, working to meet team targets
Respond to new prospect enquiries quickly and professionally, ensuring every interaction leaves a positive impression
Keep client and lead data up to date with accuracy and attention to detail
Work with the Client Opportunity Leader on surveys, reporting, and insights
Collaborate with the Marketing team to support client communications and provide feedback on results
Share ideas and contribute to new ways of engaging and interacting with clients
Support wider business projects and objectives when needed
Previous experience in a customer service or business development role
Exceptional communication and influencing skills, both written and verbal
Confidence in handling client queries with professionalism
Strong organisational skills and the ability to work to tight deadlines
Attention to detail with the ability to prioritise effectively
A proactive, creative approach to engaging with clients and generating opportunities
Experience working collaboratively with wider teams and stakeholders
Hybrid working: 3 days in the office, 2 days wherever you work best
Performance bonus + Company-wide bonus (when Eight hits targets)
28 days' holiday + bank holidays + your birthday off
Private medical insurance & life assurance (4x salary)
Wellbeing support: EAP, mental health helpline, cycle-to-work scheme
Free mortgage and pension advice from our in-house experts
Volunteering time: 8 paid hours annually via The Eight Foundation
Regular socials & epic events
ai_core_responsibilities: The Client Coordinator will build and maintain strong relationships with existing clients and ensure they feel supported. Additionally, they will book appointments for clients and prospects and collaborate with various teams to achieve business objectives.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have previous experience in customer service or business development roles and possess exceptional communication skills. Strong organizational skills and a proactive approach to client engagement are also essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Multi-Award winning Principal Partner Practice of St. James's Place Wealth Management
linkedin_org_locations: 39a Commercial Road, Totton, Hampshire SO40 3YN, GB
222-224 Lower Blandford Road, Broadstone, England BH18 8DX, GB
Unit B8, Speldhurst Business Park, Speldhurst, Kent TN3 0AQ, GB
Office 8, 40 Parkway, Chelmsford, Essex CM2 7PN, GB
linkedin_org_description:
At EIGHT Financial Advice, a multi-award winning Principal Partner Practice of St. James's Place Wealth Management, we have a wide range of expertise and knowledge, developed over many years, to help people - both individually and in business - fulfil their aims and aspirations, as well as offering that all important 'peace of mind'​ for the future.

Putting our clients firmly at the heart of everything we do enables us to run a genuinely client-focused business. In our experience, no two clients are the same and we therefore ensure that we understand your personal or business objectives and deliver solutions that are right for you. 

  We aim to deliver our commitment by:
- Providing personal face to face financial advice
- Giving you the opportunity to review your financial affairs regularly
- Effectively managing your investments via our distinctive approach
- Keeping you informed of the latest news 
- Listening to your feedback
- Offering you seminars and events 

We do not provide off the shelf solutions, but we offer the benefit of a single relationship for all of your financial planning needs. We strive to offer the very best service to develop great relationships.

The value of an investment with St. James's Place will be directly linked to the performance of the funds you select and the value can therefore go down as well as up. You may get back less than you invested. 

EIGHT Financial Advice is an Appointed Representative of and represents only St. James's Place Wealth Management Plc (which is authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority) for the purpose of advising solely on the Group’s wealth management products and services, more details of which are set out on the Group’s website at https://www.sjp.co.uk/products.  The St. James’s Place Partnership and the titles ‘Partner’ and ‘Partner Practice’ are marketing terms used to describe St. James’s Place representatives.

EIGHT Financial Advice is a trading name of Eight Wealth Management.

title: Freelance - Executive/Virtual Assistant - Remote
description_text:
The People Group is a fractional talent and support business focused on connecting exceptional professionals with growing founders and leadership teams. We pride ourselves on quality, consistency, and long term partnerships. We believe that when our contractors are treated with respect, clarity, and fairness, that standard naturally carries through to the clients they support. Clear expectations, open communication, and mutual accountability are central to how we work.
We are currently seeking a highly skilled and well organized Executive Assistant to join our talent pool on a freelance basis. In this role, you will provide high level administrative and operational support to founders and senior leaders, helping ensure their day to day operations run efficiently and effectively. This is a fully remote role, supporting clients across different time zones, and is ideal for someone comfortable working independently in a fractional setup.
Responsibilities
Manage calendars, schedule meetings and appointments for clients
Coordinate travel arrangements and accommodations
Prepare and edit documents, reports, and presentations
Handle phone calls, emails, and other correspondence
Organize and maintain files, records, and databases
Assist in the preparation of meeting agendas and take minutes
Collaborate with other team members to ensure efficient workflow
Arrange and coordinate events and conferences
Perform ad-hoc administrative tasks as needed
Respond to and manage client requests, including ad hoc tasks, with professionalism and attention to detail
Proven experience as an Executive Assistant, Personal Assistant, or similar role
Experience working in a fractional, freelance, or contract based role
Exceptional organizational and time-management skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
Proficient in MS Office and Google Suite
Ability to multitask and prioritize tasks effectively
Strong attention to detail and accuracy
High degree of professionalism and confidentiality
Ability to work independently and remotely
Flexible schedule and availability
Demonstrated experience supporting multiple executives or clients simultaneously
Experience with project management tools
Familiarity with video conferencing and online collaboration tools
Knowledge of office management systems and procedures
Experience working with HNW individuals preferable but not essential. 
Experience working with Family Offices preferable but not essential. 
Experience working with Business Leaders preferable but not essential. 
Flexible with occasionally meeting clients in person if requested. 
Knowledgeable about emerging AI software, tools, models, and methodologies that challenge the conventional approach to executive assistance.
You will be working as a contractor under the The People Group company name, therefore you are not a direct employee and only work as a contractor for our business. 
The benefit of this structure means, all of our clients sign contracts directly withThe People Group and not you as the Contractor, therefore you are protected as a remote contractor as The People Group take cares of the client service side of the function.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Executive Assistant will provide high-level administrative and operational support to founders and senior leaders, ensuring day-to-day operations run efficiently. Responsibilities include managing calendars, coordinating travel, preparing documents, and responding to client requests.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have proven experience as an Executive Assistant or similar role, preferably in a fractional or freelance capacity. Exceptional organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work independently are essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: A US Holding Company specialising in the acquisition of Fractional Staffing businesses across the Globe.
linkedin_org_description:
The People Group acquires and scales businesses specialising in the Fractional Staffing industries. 

Current acquisitions; 

JamPot Support
Byron 
Leverage Assistant

Manage calendars, schedule meetings and appointments for clients
Coordinate travel arrangements and accommodations
Prepare and edit documents, reports, and presentations
Handle phone calls, emails, and other correspondence
Organize and maintain files, records, and databases
Assist in the preparation of meeting agendas and take minutes
Collaborate with other team members to ensure efficient workflow
Arrange and coordinate events and conferences
Perform ad-hoc administrative tasks as needed
Respond to and manage client requests, including ad hoc tasks, with professionalism and attention to detail
Proven experience as an Executive Assistant, Personal Assistant, or similar role
Experience working in a fractional, freelance, or contract based role
Exceptional organizational and time-management skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
Proficient in MS Office and Google Suite
Ability to multitask and prioritize tasks effectively
Strong attention to detail and accuracy
High degree of professionalism and confidentiality
Ability to work independently and remotely
Flexible schedule and availability
Demonstrated experience supporting multiple executives or clients simultaneously
Experience with project management tools
Familiarity with video conferencing and online collaboration tools
Knowledge of office management systems and procedures
Experience working with HNW individuals preferable but not essential. 
Experience working with Family Offices preferable but not essential. 
Experience working with Business Leaders preferable but not essential. 
Flexible with occasionally meeting clients in person if requested. 
Knowledgeable about emerging AI software, tools, models, and methodologies that challenge the conventional approach to executive assistance.
You will be working as a contractor under the The People Group company name, therefore you are not a direct employee and only work as a contractor for our business. 
The benefit of this structure means, all of our clients sign contracts directly withThe People Group and not you as the Contractor, therefore you are protected as a remote contractor as The People Group take cares of the client service side of the function.
