Sonar is the trust and verification layer for AI code, and the industry standard for automated code review for 17+ years. 

Sonar delivers deterministic, repeatable, and actionable code verification at scale by integrating code quality and code security into a single platform. The company analyzes more than 750 billion lines of code daily to ensure software is secure, reliable, and maintainable. 

Sonar is rooted in the open source community and is trusted by 7M+ developers globally, including teams at Snowflake, Booking.com, Deutsche Bank, AstraZeneca, and Ford Motor Company. 

To learn more about Sonar, please visit: www.sonar.com

title: Associate Director of Multilingual Learner Programs
description_text:
At Rocketship Public Schools, we believe in the infinite possibility of human potential. We believe that every student deserves the right to dream, to discover, and to develop their unique potential and it is our responsibility and our privilege to unleash the potential inside every Rocketeer we serve. Our non-profit network of public elementary charter schools propels student achievement, develops exceptional educators, and partners with parents who enable high-quality public schools to thrive in their community. We are a collective of parents, teachers, leaders, and students working together to transform the future for underserved communities across our country. At Rocketship Public Schools, we are unleashing potential.

Position Description

The Coaching and Curriculum team is seeking an experienced and highly skilled Associate Director of Multilingual Learners (MLL) Programs to support high-quality, rigorous, and equitable language development and literacy instruction for multilingual learners across Rocketship Tennessee schools.

This role partners closely with Tennessee regional and school leaders to strengthen instructional practice, ensure effective implementation of multilingual learner programs, and improve outcomes for emergent bilingual students. The Associate Director of Multilingual Learners provides direct instructional leadership and coaching support to 3–5 Tennessee schools, ensuring alignment between language development, literacy instruction, and Rocketship’s Core Model. The role works closely with regional leadership and school-based instructional teams and reports to the Senior Director of Schools. 

Below are the essential functions:
\n

Coaching & Leader Development
Build instructional capacity among Tennessee school leaders and instructional leaders to effectively serve multilingual learners.
Provide direct coaching to school leaders and instructional leaders focused on language development, sheltered instruction, and culturally responsive practices
Observe classroom instruction and planning, delivering actionable feedback aligned to multilingual learner best practices and Rocketship’s instructional vision
Partner with leaders to strengthen their ability to coach teachers supporting emergent bilingual students

Program Implementation & Fidelity
Ensure strong implementation of Rocketship’s multilingual learner program and language development model across supported Tennessee schools.
Ensure strong implementation of Rocketship’s multilingual learner program, including oversight of Individualized Language Plans (ILPs), progress monitoring, and instructional fidelity across supported Tennessee schools.
Monitor and support compliance with state, district, and federal requirements, including ML identification, enrollment, documentation, and reporting timelines.
Lead regional coordination and compliance for ELPA21 assessment planning, administration, and monitoring in alignment with district and TDOE expectations.
Partner with school leaders, district teams, and SIS partners to ensure accurate placement, data integrity, and high-quality multilingual services.
Develop and curate pacing guidance, tools, exemplars, and professional learning resources to strengthen multilingual instruction and implementation.


Progress Monitoring & Instructional Effectiveness
Monitor the effectiveness of multilingual learner program implementation and student language development outcomes.
Drive on data driven action planning and use of language proficiency assessments, literacy data, and classroom observation tools
Analyze priority data to identify trends in language acquisition, instructional effectiveness, and program implementation
Share clear insights and recommendations with Tennessee regional leaders to inform coaching, planning, and targeted support

Regional Collaboration, Vision & Goal-Setting
Partner with Tennessee regional leadership to align on vision, priorities, and goals for multilingual learner programming.
Collaborate with regional leaders to define and refine the Tennessee multilingual learner instructional vision and success metrics
Support schools in setting goals for language development and monitoring progress toward shared outcomes
Partner with regional teams and the broader Academics team to ensure alignment between multilingual learner programming, literacy instruction, and Rocketship’s Core Model

Professional Development & Regional Learning
Design and facilitate professional learning experiences focused on high-quality instruction for multilingual learners.
Design and lead engaging professional development for Tennessee teachers, school leaders, and instructional staff on language development and instructional best practices
Identify regional professional learning priorities using qualitative and quantitative data
Support and lead on summer professional development and start-of-year learning experiences for Tennessee schools in addition to yearlong development opportunities at both a regional and school level

Core Characteristics
Successful candidates will demonstrate Rocketship’s core characteristics in their leadership and instructional practice:
Pursuit of Excellence – Continuously improves outcomes through data, feedback, and reflective practice
Authenticity – Leads with transparency, humility, and alignment to Rocketship’s values
Community – Builds strong relationships and values collective impact across schools and teams
Tenacity – Relentlessly pursues instructional goals in service of student success
Innovation – Challenges the status quo and takes responsible risks to improve instruction

Qualifications
Experience
3–5 years of progressively responsible experience in multilingual learner programming, curriculum development, instructional leadership, or school administration
At least 3 years of K–5 lead teaching experience, preferably including multilingual learners, with evidence of strong student outcomes
At least 1 year of experience coaching and leading adults in a school or instructional leadership role
Experience implementing and coaching on high-quality language development or literacy programs for multilingual learners preferred
Skills
Strong instructional coaching and adult learning facilitation skills
Deep understanding of language acquisition, culturally responsive instruction, and inclusive instructional practices
Strong analytical skills with the ability to use data to drive instructional decisions
Strong communication skills
Excellent organizational and project management skills
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Ability to influence without direct authority
Proficiency with Google Workspace
Education
Bachelor’s degree required
Master’s degree preferred (Education, TESOL, Bilingual Education, or a related field)

\n
$90,000 - $112,500 a year
\n
Physical Demands:
The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this position. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the functions. While performing the duties of this position, the employee is regularly required to talk, hear, and communicate with both adults and children. The employee frequently is required to use hands or fingers, handle or feel objects, tools, or controls. The employee is frequently required to stand; walk; sit; and reach with hands and arms. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 50 pounds and occasionally climb ladders. Specific vision abilities required by this position include close vision, distance vision, and the ability to adjust focus. The noise level in the work environment is moderate to high.

Compensation:
Commensurate with qualifications and experience, plus excellent health and wellness benefits, 403b retirement plan, flexible spending account (FSA), and generous paid time off. 

Rocketship Public Schools provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, marital status, amnesty, or status as a covered veteran in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws. Rocketship Public Schools complies with applicable state and local laws governing non-discrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including, but not limited to, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

Rocketship Public Schools expressly prohibits any form of unlawful employee harassment based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, or veteran status. Improper interference with the ability of Rocketship Public Schools’ employees to perform their expected job duties is absolutely not tolerated. Click here for our Sexual Harassment Policy. For questions, concerns, or complaints, please contact Human Resources.

title: Category Operations & Enablement Specialist
description_text:
MOO set out to shake up the world of print back in 2006. And we’ve come a long way since. Today we’re a 400 + strong team (we’re thinking of getting matching jackets) who create print and digital products for companies of all sizes – from Google and AirBnB to the guy who makes those amazing prints you found at a craft fair. 
Our global HQ is in London, UK, while we also have premises in Dagenham. In the US, you’ll find us in Boston, MA, as well as East Providence, RI and Denver, CO and now in Cape Town South Africa! 

The Category Operations & Enablement Specialist supports MOO’s Category Managers and GTM team by owning the day-to-day operational execution and enablement work that keeps categories running smoothly and launches on track.

Please note - This role will be based either in our London HQ or Cape Town, South Africa 
\n

The Role
This role sits at the intersection of category operations, product readiness, and commercial execution. 
You’ll manage SKU and availability hygiene, monitor stock and pricing signals, coordinate creative and product readiness tasks, and produce clear documentation such as FAQs and competitor summaries.

Responsibilities 
Category Operations & Admin Support
Provide hands-on operational and administrative support to Category Managers across Print, Stickers, and Merch.
Monitor stock, pricing, and availability, with particular focus on third-party products.
Flag out-of-stock (OOS) and pricing issues to Category Managers and relevant Operations partners. Support pricing updates and checks as directed by Category Managers.
Maintain accurate SKU setup and product status in the PIM and related product systems:
Adding and updating SKUs
Managing OOS and availability flags
Ensuring product data accuracy across systems

Product Readiness & Launch Enablement
Submit briefs and tickets for functional and product photography.
Organise and track product samples, customisation guidelines, and supporting product information.
Upload, organise, and share final creative and product assets with relevant teams.
Support end-to-end product testing, including files, ordering, and basic QA checks to ensure readiness.
Help ensure product changes and launches are operationally ready from a data and documentation perspective.

Documentation & FAQs
Write and maintain product FAQs, internal reference materials, and customer-ready explanations.
Support creation and upkeep of category and product documentation used across teams.
Ensure information is clear, accurate, and consistent across internal tools and shared resources.

Competitive & Market Insight
Conduct ongoing competitor research across Print, Stickers, Merch, and Promotional Products.
Produce routine competitor highlights summarising new launches, pricing changes, and promotions.
Maintain competitor comparison materials to support category, pricing, and assortment discussions.

Cross-Functional Coordination

title: Ecommerce Content Writer
description_text:
MOO set out to shake up the world of print back in 2006. And we’ve come a long way since. Today we’re a 400 + strong team (we’re thinking of getting matching jackets) who create print and digital products for companies of all sizes – from Google and AirBnB to the guy who makes those amazing prints you found at a craft fair. 
Our global HQ is in London, UK, while we also have premises in Dagenham. In the US, you’ll find us in Boston, MA, as well as East Providence, RI and Denver, CO and now in Cape Town South Africa! 

The Ecommerce team at are the forefront of MOO's digital experience, working cross-functionally with Creative, Tech, and Commercial teams to ensure every interaction on moo.com is as premium as our products. We balance brand storytelling with data-driven decision-making to turn visitors into lifelong customers. As the business continues to invest in new technology with a focus on enhancing the digital experience and improving organic discoverability, we are looking for a dedicated writer to join our ranks.
\n

The Role
So, what will you actually be doing? Your mission is to be the voice of MOO across our digital storefront. 
You will be responsible for crafting high-converting, user-centric copy that guides customers through the funnel - from the first click on a search engine to the final "Confirm Order" button. 
You’ll bridge the gap between our brand's playful personality and the functional requirements of SEO and UX, ensuring our site is both a joy to read and a breeze to navigate.

About You
We’re looking for a wordsmith who can think like a strategist. 
You should be someone who obsesses over the "why" behind a headline and understands that sometimes, a single word change can transform a conversion rate. 
You have a passion for UX writing principles and a solid grasp of how SEO impacts the bottom line. 
A creative problem-solver who enjoys the challenge of writing for both humans and algorithms.
A digital-first writer who understands how people read and interact with content on screens.
Highly organised with the ability to manage multiple projects and stakeholders in a fast-paced environment.
Comfortable communicating changes across multiple teams, with the ability to educate on recommendations and best practices.

Responsibilities 
You will be the lead for all on-site copy, working hand-in-hand with our commercial and technical teams to optimise the customer journey. 
You’ll translate complex product features into compelling benefits while keeping technical SEO requirements front and centre.
Write and optimise high-quality copy for product pages, landing pages, and category headers that aligns with MOO’s brand voice. - Collaborate with UX/UI designers to create intuitive microcopy (buttons, tooltips, and checkout flows) that reduces friction and improves the user experience.
Execute SEO strategies utilising keyword research, integrating terms naturally into metadata and on-page content to drive organic traffic.
Support Technical Ecommerce by auditing site content for clarity and accuracy, ensuring a consistent experience across all regions.
Partner with Commercial teams to craft persuasive copy for seasonal campaigns, promotions, and new product launches.
Iterate and test copy variants based on data from A/B tests and user feedback to constantly improve site performance.

Nice To Haves
Experience working within a CMS (Content Management System).
Experience working with iterative testing or experimentation programmes.
Experience working with AI or a proactive attitude with how AI can help shape the future of writing content at MOO

\n

title: Category Manager, Sourced & Promotional Products
locations_derived: East Providence, Rhode Island, United States
description_text:
MOO set out to shake up the world of print back in 2006. And we’ve come a long way since. Today we’re a 400 + strong team (we’re thinking of getting matching jackets) who create print and digital products for companies of all sizes – from Google and AirBnB to the guy who makes those amazing prints you found at a craft fair. 
Our global HQ is in London, UK, while we also have premises in Dagenham. In the US, you’ll find us in Boston, MA Denver, CO, and East Providence, RI

We’re making new and exciting things – and we could really do with your help. 
\n

The Role
This role leads the day-to-day category management of MOO’s sourced and promotional product assortment, working with third-party suppliers to deliver branded merchandise that reflects customer demand, current promo trends, and MOO’s design-led brand ethos.
You will bring a finger-on-the-pulse understanding of the promo industry — spotting emerging products, materials, and decoration techniques — and translate those insights into a focused, commercially sound assortment.
In parallel, this role partners closely with MOO’s existing Stock, Planning, and Operations teams to support and improve inventory performance, helping ensure sourced and internally manufactured merch products are available, efficient, and revenue-supporting without disrupting established ownership.

Responsibilities 
Promotional Product & Assortment Management
Lead the management and evolution of MOO’s sourced and promotional product range.
Blend promo industry trends, customer demand, and MOO’s brand and design standards to shape the assortment.
Monitor product performance and recommend assortment updates, additions, and exits.
Support identification of promo products with potential to evolve into hybrid or in-house offerings.

Trend & Market Insight
Maintain a strong point of view on promo product trends, including materials, sustainability, decoration techniques, and emerging use cases.
Stay closely connected to supplier innovation, trade shows, and industry developments.
Share market insight with Category, Marketing, and Design partners to inform launches and campaigns.

Third-Party Supplier Management
Manage relationships with third-party promo suppliers, acting as the primary category contact.
Support negotiations on pricing, MOQs, lead times, decoration capabilities, and service levels.
Establish scalable internal supplier onboarding processes that reduce friction and accelerate access to new promotional products.
Track supplier performance and collaborate on continuous improvement.

Inventory Enablement (Across Merch)
Partner with Stock and Planning teams to support improvements in availability, stock turns, and OOS reduction.
Provide category insight, promotional drivers, and demand context to support forecasting and replenishment decisions.
Collaborate with existing owners to identify out-of-stock risks, excess exposure, and lifecycle decisions.
Support refinement of inventory policies (weeks of cover, safety stock, replenishment cadence) in partnership with Stock and Planning teams.

Cross-Functional Collaboration
Work closely with the Internal Manufacturing Category Manager to align demand drivers and roadmap timing with inventory readiness.
Partner with Marketing to support launch readiness and promo storytelling.
Act as a collaborative category voice with Operations and Supply Chain, supporting continuous improvement initiatives.

About You
Demonstrated experience in promotional products and branded merchandise
Hands-on experience working with promo suppliers, MOQs, lead times, and decoration methods
Strong understanding of promo industry trends and market dynamics
Commercial mindset with comfort using data to inform decisions
Experience supporting inventory planning or demand forecasting
Collaborative working style and ability to influence without authority

Nice To Haves
Experience working with design-led or premium brands
Exposure to hybrid or decorated promo products
Experience working with PromoStandards

\n

We also offer a 401(k) with company match and no vesting schedule, a dog-friendly and casual office environment, and free lunches every other week. Because when you feel good, your work does too.

title: AI Programmer
description_text:
At Avalanche Studios Group, we are looking for an experienced AI Programmer who will work on our successful title theHunter: Call of the Wild, based out of our studios in Stockholm and Malmö.
Initially released in 2017, Call of the Wild has been supported with many successful DLCs and content releases. Now, with being live on 5 platforms we continue to improve the quality and experience for our huge player base by adding new features (gameplay, visuals, stability, performance, UI/UX).

The Position

As an AI Gameplay Programmer, you will work side by side with designers, animators and gameplay programmers to realize complex and realistic animal AI behavior. Take part in designing and implementing their behavior by using and extending existing tools as well as implementing game systems related to pathfinding, decision-making, group movement and locomotion. This role will require you to take high-level design ideas and translate them into practical solutions.

It is expected that you see your responsibility as delivering a successful end-user experience, together with your teammates, rather than just handing off technological solutions to content creators. Much of the success of well-executed animal characters is down to how successfully they are integrated with all of the systems within the game engine, e.g. physics, animation, UI, SFX, VFX etc. Previous experience and proven ability to work with multiple systems and stakeholders is a definite advantage. Finally, as with any other programming role, you will have to be able to balance functional requirements with technical requirements in the areas of performance, stability, memory usage, and interoperability.
\n

What you'll do
Work side by side with designers, animators and gameplay and animation programmers to realize complex and realistic animal AI behavior.
Take part in designing and implementing animal behavior by using and extending existing tools for behaviour trees and navigation.
Implement game systems related to spawning, pathfinding, decision-making, group movement and locomotion.
Take high-level design ideas and translate them into practical solutions.

Who you are
A good understanding of what makes games fun
A passion for creating solid game AI
At least 3 years of Industry Experience, preferably on bespoke game engines
Work with different AI architectures and techniques e.g behavior trees and pathfinding
Able to write readable, robust and performant code in C++ 
Experience working with animation state machines, animation blending, IK and similar techniques
A good understanding of all aspects of game programming in multiplayer games
Prior experience working as a programmer on at least one shipped title on current or previous generation of consoles
Prior experience with Havok AI / Havok Navigation is a plus
Strong communication & verbal skills in English

\n
Note: Research shows under-represented groups in the industry might not apply to a job if they don’t check off every requirement. At Avalanche, we’re more about your drive to learn and interest in tackling challenges than what’s on your resume! So don’t stress if you don’t tick every box— shoot us your application anyway! Diverse opinions and different experiences are what make our teams so great, not itemized lists of qualifications.

The Malmö location
You’ll find us in a standout building from 1958, just a stone’s throw from the Central Station. Within these walls, the American dream was leveraged to sell muscle cars more than half a century ago. Today, the location balances Avalanche Studios Group’s industrial, high-octane heritage with a bright and homey vibe featuring resident dogs and patio-grown chilies.

Our values
At Avalanche Studios Group, we create worlds beyond limits. But that’s not exclusive to our games, so we’re committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. All Avalanchers have a shared responsibility to create an open work environment where everyone is treated equally and respectfully. Being part of our world is not contingent on your heritage, your gender, or sexual orientation. It’s all about your passion and creativity.

How to apply
To apply for this position, please register below and provide your CV in English. We review applications continuously. All further studio-related information is provided under a non-disclosure agreement.

At Avalanche Studios Group we're always on the lookout for talented individuals to join our passionate team, but please note that relocation assistance is not available for this role.
ai_core_responsibilities: The AI Programmer will collaborate with designers, animators, and other programmers to create complex and realistic animal AI behavior, focusing on designing and implementing systems for pathfinding, decision-making, and group movement. This involves translating high-level design ideas into practical, integrated solutions across various game engine systems.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have at least 3 years of industry experience, preferably with bespoke game engines, and be proficient in writing robust, performant C++ code. Essential technical knowledge includes working with AI architectures like behavior trees and pathfinding, alongside experience with animation techniques such as state machines and IK.

Avalanche Studios Group is committed to being a truly inclusive and sustainable workplace that creates innovative games through the power of our people’s creativity. title: Front Office Agent - Aethos Monterosa
description_text:
Who We Are

Extraordinary places, passionate people, and shared learning experiences: this is Aethos. We believe that traveling and hospitality should leave a lasting positive impact on the mind, body, and soul. That's the reason why we choose our locations very carefully and work together with people who want to be part of a community instead of just ”having a job”. Everyone who works at Aethos can make a personal impact on the community and company - great ideas can come from anywhere. 

We are a young and dynamic hospitality company that understands the value of time with friends and loved ones. We make a conscious choice towards sustainability and wellbeing, and the luxury of time and space. We stand for freedom from the stresses and anxieties of modern life. We want to be the first choice for anyone who seeks respite and refuge from the boring, the stressful, and the unsustainable. That's why we define hospitality differently: while all of our decisions should be made with the community and needs of our guests in mind, we always thrive to create a community that we want to be part of and that improves the world around us. We invite you to be part of our story.

About Aethos Monterosa

In the middle of the Aosta Valley, below the Monte Rosa and Cervino glaciers, lies Aethos Monterosa – a five-star active luxury resort featuring two F&B outlets, a SPA, and its own climbing and ice climbing wall. We are looking for an outstanding team to join us for the upcoming summer season.
The essence of great hospitality is always an exceptional team — that’s why we are looking for you!
\n

The Essence of the Role:
Guest Welcomer: Have you ever experienced the joy of making someone feel truly welcome? Get ready to experience that satisfaction every day. As our Front Office Agent, you'll be the ambassador of our hotel's hospitality, greeting guests with a warm smile and a friendly demeanor, and ensuring that their first impression of our establishment is a positive and memorable one.
Check-In Champion: Are you ready to be the master of efficiency and organization at our front desk? From processing check-ins and check-outs to assisting with room assignments and reservations, you'll be the linchpin that ensures our guests' arrival and departure experiences are smooth and hassle-free.
Information Guru: Do you thrive on providing helpful information and assistance to others? Then you'll feel right at home in our front office, where you'll be the go-to resource for guests seeking information about our hotel amenities, local attractions, and dining options, ensuring that every guest has everything they need for a comfortable and enjoyable stay.
Problem-Solving Pro: Ready to tackle any challenge that comes your way with grace and professionalism? With your quick thinking and resourcefulness, you'll handle guest inquiries and requests with ease, ensuring that any issues are resolved promptly and to the guest's satisfaction.

Your qualifications to join the adventure:
A passion for hospitality that shines through in everything you do.
Excellent interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage guests and make them feel welcome and valued.
Strong organizational skills, allowing you to multitask and prioritize effectively in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, allowing you to convey information clearly and effectively to guests and colleagues.
An unwavering dedication to providing exceptional service, with a commitment to going above and beyond to ensure that every guest's needs are met and exceeded.
Oh, and did we mention a sense of professionalism? Because in our front office, professionalism is not just a requirement—it's a way of life.
Italian and english is a must

What We Offer:
Professional development and training opportunities, with possibilities for growth within the Aethos brand
Special rates on stays and services at our hotels


\n
We are very much looking forward to your application.
Application should be in English.

Special rates on stays and services at our hotels

This role also requires serving as the primary information resource for guests regarding amenities and local attractions while promptly resolving any issues.

title: BI Analyst
description_text:
Ajax Systems — міжнародна технологічна компанія і найбільший у Європі виробник охоронних систем. Продуктам Ajax довіряють уже понад 4,5 мільйони кінцевих користувачів і 330 тисяч PRO-користувачів у більш ніж 180 країнах. Компанія пропонує комплексні рішення для захисту житлових і комерційних об'єктів різного масштабу. Нині портфоліо Ajax налічує 280 пристроїв для захисту від вторгнення, відеоспостереження, пожежної безпеки та комфорту й автоматизації. Кожного року ми демонструємо кратне зростання як у чисельності команди, так і в кількості користувачів у всьому світі.

Наразі ми шукаємо спеціаліста з аналітики даних, який будує автоматизовані звіти й пайплайни та допомагає бізнесу приймати рішення на основі даних.
\n

Обов‘язки:
Збір та отримання даних: бази даних, ERP, Google Sheets, API
Очищення та перетворення даних: Data Cleaning/Wrangling
Створення звітів та дашбордів
Візуалізація даних: вміння правильно підібрати візуалізаціі для ефективної передачі інформації
Оперативна підтримка бізнесу: відповіді на ад-hoc запити від відділів та підтримка споживачів репортів
Документування: опис методологій аналізу, джерел даних, логіки розрахунків KPI
Автоматизація звітів: автоматизація регулярної звітності та зменшення ручної операційної роботи пайплайни за допомогою інструментів ETL або функцій планувальників у BI-інструментах.

Вимоги:
Глибокий SQL-аналіз: Написання складних запитів з використанням JOIN, CTE, window functions для отримання необхідних вибірок
Побудова дата-пайплайнів та ETL-процесів: Проектування та впровадження надійних процесів завантаження, перетворення та завантаження даних з різних джерел в єдине сховище (data warehouse, data lake)
Робота з хмарними платформами: досвід роботи з Google BigQuery, Databricks
Data Storytelling: вміння не просто показати графік, а розповісти переконливу історію на основі даних, яка веде до конкретних бізнес-рішень. Включає структурування презентацій та комунікацію.
Знання Power BI6: Tech Steck: SQL, PowerBI, Google BigQuery, Databricks, Python

Ми пропонуємо: 
Роботу у вмотивованій команді та zero bullshit culture; 
конкурентну заробітну плату;
медичне страхування;
корпоративну англійську мову;
компенсацію профільного навчання;
можливість взяти участь у проєкті бета-тесту систем безпеки Ajax — отримання та тестування нових гаджетів до їх релізу.

\n
Ajax Systems — це українська історія успіху, місце неймовірної сили та енергії.Долучайтеся, щоб зробити Made in Ukraine 🇺🇦 синонімом надійності.
ai_benefits: Opportunity to beta-test Ajax security systems
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves collecting, cleaning, and transforming data from various sources like databases, ERP, Google Sheets, and APIs to build automated reports and dashboards. The analyst will also provide operational support to the business by answering ad-hoc requests and documenting methodologies and KPIs.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess deep SQL analysis skills, including writing complex queries with JOINs, CTEs, and window functions, and experience building robust data pipelines and ETL processes. Proficiency in cloud platforms like Google BigQuery and Databricks, along with strong data storytelling abilities and knowledge of Power BI, is required.
linkedin_org_slogan: The largest manufacturer of security systems in Europe
linkedin_org_description: International tech company and the largest manufacturer of security systems in Europe

title: Bellboy - Aethos Monterosa
description_text:
Who We Are

Extraordinary places, passionate people, and shared learning experiences: this is Aethos. We believe that traveling and hospitality should leave a lasting positive impact on the mind, body, and soul. That's the reason why we choose our locations very carefully and work together with people who want to be part of a community instead of just ”having a job”. Everyone who works at Aethos can make a personal impact on the community and company - great ideas can come from anywhere. 

We are a young and dynamic hospitality company that understands the value of time with friends and loved ones. We make a conscious choice towards sustainability and wellbeing, and the luxury of time and space. We stand for freedom from the stresses and anxieties of modern life. We want to be the first choice for anyone who seeks respite and refuge from the boring, the stressful, and the unsustainable. That's why we define hospitality differently: while all of our decisions should be made with the community and needs of our guests in mind, we always thrive to create a community that we want to be part of and that improves the world around us. We invite you to be part of our story.

About Aethos Monterosa

In the middle of the Aosta Valley, below the Monte Rosa and Cervino glaciers, lies Aethos Monterosa – a five-star active luxury resort featuring two F&B outlets, a SPA, and its own climbing and ice climbing wall. We are looking for an outstanding team to join us for the upcoming summer season.
The essence of great hospitality is always an exceptional team — that’s why we are looking for you!
\n

The Essence of the Role:
Guest Greeter: Have you ever experienced the joy of making someone feel truly welcome? Get ready to experience that satisfaction every day. As our Bellboy, you'll be the first point of contact for our guests, greeting them with a warm smile and a helpful attitude, and ensuring that their arrival sets the tone for a pleasant stay.
Luggage Logistics Expert: Are you ready to be the master of efficiency and organization when it comes to handling luggage? From assisting guests with their bags to delivering them to their rooms promptly and safely, you'll be the backbone of our hotel's logistics, ensuring that every guest's belongings are handled with care and professionalism.
Local Guide: Do you thrive on providing helpful information and assistance to others? Then you'll feel right at home in our hotel, where you'll be the go-to resource for guests seeking recommendations on local attractions, restaurants, and activities, ensuring that every guest has everything they need for an enjoyable stay.
Problem-Solving Pro: Ready to tackle any challenge that comes your way with grace and professionalism? With your quick thinking and resourcefulness, you'll handle guest inquiries and requests with ease, ensuring that any issues are resolved promptly and to the guest's satisfaction.

Your qualifications to join the adventure:
A passion for hospitality that shines through in everything you do.
Excellent interpersonal skills, with the ability to make guests feel welcome and valued from the moment they arrive.
Strong organizational skills, allowing you to handle multiple tasks efficiently and prioritize effectively in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, allowing you to convey information clearly and effectively to guests and colleagues.
An unwavering dedication to providing exceptional service, with a commitment to going above and beyond to ensure that every guest's needs are met and exceeded.
Oh, and did we mention a sense of enthusiasm? Because in our hotel, enthusiasm is contagious, and we need someone who shares our passion for creating memorable experiences for our guests.

What We Offer:
Professional development and training opportunities, with possibilities for growth within the Aethos brand
Special rates on stays and services at our hotels

\n
We are very much looking forward to your application.
Application should be in English.
ai_benefits: Special rates on stays and services at our hotels
ai_core_responsibilities: The Bellboy will serve as the primary guest greeter, setting a welcoming tone upon arrival, and will manage all luggage logistics efficiently and safely throughout the guest's stay. Additionally, the role involves acting as a local guide, providing recommendations, and promptly resolving guest inquiries and requests.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a strong passion for hospitality, excellent interpersonal and communication skills, and strong organizational abilities to manage tasks effectively in a fast-paced environment. An unwavering dedication to exceptional service and a high level of enthusiasm are essential for creating memorable guest experiences.
linkedin_org_slogan: Aethos is creating a new kind of premium experience for a community of modern-day explorers.
linkedin_org_description:
Aethos has been founded on the belief that traveling and hospitality should be
an experience that leaves a positive and lasting impact on your body, mind and soul.

In the middle of the Aosta Valley, below the Monte Rosa and Cervino glaciers, lies Aethos Monterosa – a five-star active luxury resort featuring two F&B outlets, a SPA, and its own climbing and ice climbing wall. We are looking for an outstanding team to join us for the upcoming summer season.
The essence of great hospitality is always an exceptional team — that’s why we are looking for you! 
\n

Essential Job Functions and Responsibilities
Room Cleaning and Maintenance: Do you have an eye for detail and a commitment to cleanliness? You’ll be responsible for thoroughly cleaning guest rooms, including making beds, dusting, vacuuming, and sanitizing bathrooms. Your goal is to create a spotless and inviting environment for our guests.
Linen and Towel Management: Are you organized and efficient? You’ll handle the changing and replenishing of linens, towels, and other amenities, ensuring that each room is fully stocked and ready for the next guest.
Common Area Upkeep: Ready to keep our entire property looking its best? You’ll also maintain cleanliness in common areas such as hallways, lobbies, and lounges, ensuring that our hotel always presents itself in the best possible light.
Guest Requests and Service: Do you take pride in going above and beyond for guests? You’ll respond to guest requests for extra amenities, such as pillows, blankets, or toiletries, and ensure their needs are met promptly and courteously.
Inventory Management: Are you proactive and resourceful? You’ll keep track of cleaning supplies and report when inventory needs to be replenished, ensuring that you always have what you need to maintain the high standards of our hotel.
Attention to Detail: Do you take satisfaction in a job well done? You’ll conduct final inspections of rooms to make sure they meet our high standards before guests arrive, ensuring that every detail is perfect.
Team Collaboration: Are you a team player? You’ll work closely with the rest of the housekeeping team and other hotel staff, contributing to a supportive and efficient work environment.

Core Competencies
Fluent in English & Italian is a must
1+ years of hospitality experience is a plus
A passion for cleanliness and attention to detail that ensures every room is perfect.
Previous housekeeping or cleaning experience is preferred but not required.
Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage time effectively.
Physical stamina to handle the demands of the role, including standing, walking, and lifting.
A friendly and positive attitude, with a dedication to providing excellent guest service.
Good communication skills and the ability to work well with others.
A proactive and responsible approach to your work.
And a sense of pride in creating a welcoming environment for our guests.

What we offer
Professional development and training opportunities, with possibilities for growth within the Aethos brand


\n
We are very much looking forward to your application.
Application should be in English.

This role also involves managing linen and towel replenishment, maintaining common areas, responding promptly to guest requests, and tracking cleaning supply inventory.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be fluent in both English and Italian and possess a strong passion for cleanliness with meticulous attention to detail. While 1+ years of hospitality experience is a plus, the role requires strong organizational skills, physical stamina for standing and lifting, and a friendly, proactive attitude dedicated to excellent guest service.

Essential Job Functions and Responsibilities
Outlet Ownership: Are you ready to lead one of the most iconic spaces within the hotel? You’ll oversee the daily operations of the restaurant, ensuring exceptional service, smooth coordination, and a consistently high standard of hospitality that reflects the quality of the property.
Guest Experience Excellence: Do you thrive on delivering refined, memorable dining experiences? You’ll be the point of reference for all front-of-house interactions—welcoming guests, handling feedback, and making sure every moment at the table is seamless, personal, and enjoyable.
Team Leadership: Are you a leader who brings out the best in others? You’ll manage and motivate your team with confidence and care, ensuring everyone understands their role and delivers at their highest potential. You’ll create a positive, service-oriented culture, in close collaboration with the kitchen and hotel departments.
Operational Efficiency: Can you manage service flow with precision and calm under pressure? From team schedules and floor planning to service preparation and coordination, you’ll keep every detail under control to guarantee flawless operations, even at peak hours.
Sales & Performance: Do you combine service excellence with a business mindset? You’ll track performance metrics, identify improvement areas, and implement strategies to increase guest satisfaction and revenue—without compromising quality or guest experience.
Inventory & Cost Control: Are you financially aware and operationally sharp? You’ll manage stock, control orders, maintain supplier relationships, and reduce waste—ensuring that quality is upheld while staying aligned with budget targets.
Brand & Experience Representation: Can you translate brand values into every service detail?You’ll ensure the outlet reflects the hotel’s identity in every aspect—from the ambiance and presentation to the language and attitude of your team. You set the tone, always.

Core Competencies
Fluent in English and Italian – both written and spoken
Previous experience in a restaurant within a high-end hotel or similar environment
At least 1 year of experience in the hospitality sector
Passion for hospitality, food & beverage, and delivering top-level guest experiences
Proven leadership in roles such as Chef de Partie, Supervisor, or Assistant Manager
Strong organizational and time-management skills
A refined eye for detail, service style, and presentation
Solid understanding of food safety, hygiene protocols, and service standards
Stamina and resilience to handle demanding service hours
A collaborative mindset—you lead the team, but you’re part of it too

Special rates on stays and services at our hotels

Guest Welcomer: Have you ever experienced the joy of making someone feel truly welcome? Get ready to experience that satisfaction every day. As our Front Office Agent, you'll be the ambassador of our hotel's hospitality, greeting guests with a warm smile and a friendly demeanor, and ensuring that their first impression of our establishment is a positive and memorable one.
Check-In Champion: Are you ready to be the master of efficiency and organization at our front desk? From processing check-ins and check-outs to assisting with room assignments and reservations, you'll be the linchpin that ensures our guests' arrival and departure experiences are smooth and hassle-free.
Information Guru: Do you thrive on providing helpful information and assistance to others? Then you'll feel right at home in our front office, where you'll be the go-to resource for guests seeking information about our hotel amenities, local attractions, and dining options, ensuring that every guest has everything they need for a comfortable and enjoyable stay.
Problem-Solving Pro: Ready to tackle any challenge that comes your way with grace and professionalism? With your quick thinking and resourcefulness, you'll handle guest inquiries and requests with ease, ensuring that any issues are resolved promptly and to the guest's satisfaction.

Your qualifications to join the adventure:
A passion for hospitality that shines through in everything you do.
Excellent interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage guests and make them feel welcome and valued.
Strong organizational skills, allowing you to multitask and prioritize effectively in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, allowing you to convey information clearly and effectively to guests and colleagues.
An unwavering dedication to providing exceptional service, with a commitment to going above and beyond to ensure that every guest's needs are met and exceeded.
Oh, and did we mention a sense of professionalism? Because in our front office, professionalism is not just a requirement—it's a way of life.
Italian and english is a must

What We Offer:
Professional development and training opportunities, with possibilities for growth within the Aethos brand


\n
We are very much looking forward to your application.
Application should be in English.

They will also serve as the primary information resource for guests regarding amenities and local attractions, resolving issues promptly.

title: [Réseau Partenaire] Courtier en Crédit Immobilier H/F
description_text:
Pretto, c’est la fintech qui rend simple, efficace et équitable la recherche de crédit immobilier. Le groupe offre notamment aux acteurs de l’immobilier toutes les briques pour que ces derniers puissent construire leur propre offre de courtage en crédit immobilier. Dans ce cadre, Pretto recrute pour l’un de ses partenaires un(e) Courtier/tière pour lancer leur offre de conseil en crédit immobilier.

Description du poste

Dans le cadre de la création d’une activité chez l’un de nos partenaires, vous serez au cœur de son nouveau dispositif. Vous intervenez en véritable expert du financement immobilier. A ce titre, vos principales missions seront :

- D'assurer aux conseillers immo et à leurs clients une réactivité et une qualité de service irréprochables. 
- De prendre en charge des leads qualifiés transmis par le réseau (aucune prospection commerciale à prévoir !). 
- D'accompagner les clients dans l’analyse de leur situation et la construction de la solution de financement la plus adaptée à leur projet immobilier. 
- De négocier avec les partenaires bancaires afin d’obtenir les meilleures conditions de financement. 
- De développer une relation de confiance avec les clients, assurer leur fidélisation et encourager la recommandation. 

Profil recherché

• Une expérience confirmée en crédit immobilier, idéalement acquise au sein d’un réseau de courtage. 
• Une forte orientation commerciale : goût du challenge, de la performance, de l’atteinte des objectifs et de la satisfaction client. 
• Une solide expertise technique : parfaite maîtrise des produits de crédit immobilier, de la réglementation en vigueur et des mécanismes de négociation bancaire. 
• Une posture de conseil affirmée et un excellent sens du relationnel, aussi bien avec les conseillers immobiliers qu’avec les clients finaux 
• Une rigueur, une fiabilité et un bon esprit d’analyse, indispensables pour sécuriser et optimiser les dossiers 
• Une aisance avec les outils digitaux modernes et les environnements technologiques 

Ce que notre partenaire offre 
Une entreprise dynamique et challengeante.
Des projets stimulants et des missions variées.
La possibilité de développer vos compétences et d’évoluer au sein d’un groupe en croissance.
Un package de rémunération attractif avec un variable pouvant doubler votre rémunération fixe annuelle.
Prime vacances, tickets restaurants et mutuelle d'entreprise intéressante. 

Le poste est en présentiel, basé à Lieusaint (77).

\n

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: You will ensure impeccable service quality for real estate advisors and their clients while managing qualified leads. Additionally, you will negotiate with banking partners to secure optimal financing conditions.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have confirmed experience in real estate credit, ideally within a brokerage network, and a strong commercial orientation. Technical expertise in credit products and regulations, along with excellent interpersonal skills, is essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Le crédit immo, comme ça devrait être.

title: Member Experience Analyst
description_text:
Match Group is a leading provider of dating products across the globe. Our portfolio includes Tinder, Hinge, Match-Meetic, PlentyOfFish, Okcupid, The League, Archer, Salams and others, each designed to spark meaningful connections for singles worldwide. Creating a sense of belonging doesn’t stop at our products - it’s the foundation of every team we hire.

When it comes to dating, the connection starts online, but the real magic happens once you meet in real life (IRL). We think the same is true for creating the best platforms, so we work together IRL in our Paris office 3 days/week.


Role overview : 

The Member Experience Analyst will primarily support the Tinder line of business. This role focuses on analyzing customer support and Trust & Safety data to produce clear, actionable insights, and translating those insights into stories that are easy for non-technical audiences to understand.

Tinder is on a mission to bring people together by facilitating millions of meaningful connections around the world. As the business and our global teams continue to grow, data plays an increasingly important role in how we make decisions and improve the member experience.

This is a unique opportunity to support reporting and analytics within the Match Group Central Trust & Safety organization. The team works closely with operations, product, and engineering to surface insights that help improve tools, processes, and outcomes for both members and internal teams. You will have mentorship and opportunities to grow your technical and analytical skills while contributing meaningfully to real, high-impact work.

\n

Your Impact : 
Build and maintain automated reports for the Trust & Safety organization to track performance, share insights broadly, and highlight progress
Partner with Trust & Safety stakeholders and Tinder Analytics to ensure reporting and metrics are ready to support new product launches and operational changes
Act as a point of contact for sharing customer insights across Tinder, helping amplify the voice of the member through data
Analyze trends in support and Trust & Safety data to identify gaps, risks, or inefficiencies in current processes
Collaborate with cross-functional partners to define, monitor, and improve operational metrics and reporting standards
Take ownership of analyses from initial question through final insight, following through to ensure findings are clearly understood and actionable
Manage multiple requests and priorities in a fast-moving environment while maintaining attention to detail

We Could Be a Match If : 
You have a Bachelor’s degree and are comfortable working with SQL to explore and analyze data
You enjoy digging into data to answer business questions, not just building dashboards, and can explain your findings clearly
You are able to think critically, troubleshoot issues, and stay composed when things do not go as planned
You communicate clearly and confidently, both in writing and in conversations with stakeholders
You have hands-on experience building and maintaining reports or dashboards in tools like Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization platforms
You enjoy working collaboratively and are comfortable partnering closely with operations, product, and analytics teams

Preferred Qualifications : 
One or more years of experience in a data analytics or related role
Experience working with customer support or operations teams
Familiarity with Zendesk Explore
Exposure to the product or software development lifecycle

Interview Process : 
Recruiter (30 min): Motivation and overview with Fatima-Ezzahra Abidi.
Hiring Manager (30 min): Deep dive on hard skills and experience with Isaac.
Technical (60 min): SQL assessment with a team member.
Behavioral (45 min): Culture and soft skills with a team member.
Final (45 min): Closing interview with a European stakeholder.

\n
Why Match Group?
• Work on products that impact millions, collaborating with global and diverse teams, shaping relationships and connections worldwide
• Benefit from a healthy work-life balance in a flexible and welcoming environment
• Enjoy a modern Paris office with a rooftop, close to transport and amenities
• Access Edenred lunch vouchers, a discounted ClassPass subscription, premium healthcare with WTW, and our Worklife sustainable mobility package
• Grow through e-learning, conferences, and knowledge-sharing sessions
• Thrive in a dynamic, agile tech environment with evolving microservices and a culture of knowledge sharing and autonomy.
• Participate in hackathons and drive innovative projects
• Join regular social events like breakfasts, lunches, and apéros 
• Use our products for free


Match Group is an equal opportunity employer and we value the rich dynamics that diversity brings to our group.

If you require reasonable accommodation to complete a job application, pre-employment testing, or a job interview or to otherwise participate in the hiring process, please contact your recruiter.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Member Experience Analyst will analyze customer support and Trust & Safety data to produce actionable insights. They will collaborate with cross-functional teams to improve tools, processes, and outcomes for members and internal teams.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a Bachelor's degree and be comfortable working with SQL for data analysis. Experience in data analytics or related roles, particularly with customer support or operations teams, is preferred.
linkedin_org_description:
Match Group is on a mission to spark meaningful connections for every single person worldwide. Our portfolio of brands—including Tinder, Hinge, Match, OKCupid and more—meets people wherever they are in their relationship journey, increasing the likelihood of great dates and genuine connection.
Since pioneering online dating over 20 years ago, hundreds of millions of people from all ages, races, genders, sexual orientations, and backgrounds have used our apps to find meaningful relationships. Today, one-third of marriages begin on one of our platforms. We remain on the cutting edge of bringing people together, revolutionizing the way they connect across more than 40 languages worldwide.
Learn more about our brands at www.mtch.com.

title: Financial Analytics Consultant
locations_derived: Capital City of Prague, Prague, Czechia
description_text:
Keboola raised $32M (Central Europe’s largest Series A in 2023) to redefine how companies work with data. Our platform delivers 10x results for customers and now we’re scaling globally to help every data team move faster, think smarter, and build with impact.

We're looking for someone who speaks the language of CFOs and finance teams—someone who's lived the challenges of consolidation, close processes, and FP&A workflows, and now wants to help others solve them.

As a Financial Analytics Consultant at Keboola, you'll be the trusted advisor who leads discovery conversations, designs solutions from a business perspective, and helps prospects see how our Financial Intelligence platform addresses their specific challenges. You won't just demo software—you'll consult on how to solve real financial analytics problems.
\n

What You'll Do
Lead Discovery
Run business discovery sessions with finance stakeholders—CFOs, VPs of Finance, Controllers, FP&A teams
Ask the questions that uncover real challenges, not just surface-level requirements
Build relationships with senior finance executives as a peer, not a vendor
Design Solutions
Translate business requirements into solution concepts that our Data Solutions Engineers can implement
Present Keboola's Financial Intelligence solution in the context of each prospect's specific use cases
Document clear requirements that minimize churn during implementation
Shape Our Offering
Contribute to positioning, content, and messaging for Financial Intelligence
Create thought leadership content—blog posts, webinars, case studies
Support key accounts with ongoing advisory on financial analytics best practices

What We're Looking For
Experience: 4+ years in FP&A, financial systems, or finance transformation roles
Background (in priority order):FP&A analysts/managers from mid-to-enterprise companies who've done consolidation and reportingFinancial systems analysts who've implemented EPM/CPM toolsBig Four advisory—specifically Finance Transformation or Performance Improvement (not audit)Finance consultants from boutique firms focused on CFO services
Mindset: You're curious about how things work. You enjoy explaining complex topics simply. You're ready to move from "doing the work" to "helping others do it better."

Must-Haves (Non-Negotiable)
Financial Domain Expertise You deeply understand financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow), consolidation processes, and FP&A workflows including budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis. You've lived this—you didn't just read about it. You know what keeps finance teams up at night because you've been there.
Hands-on Data Experience You've worked with data beyond Excel. You can write SQL. You've built reports, analyses, or models pulling from databases or data warehouses. You understand what "good data" looks like and why it matters.
Stakeholder Engagement You can hold a conversation with a CFO as a peer. You ask good questions, listen actively, and synthesize what you hear into actionable insights. Senior finance executives don't intimidate you—they energize you.
English Proficiency Professional working proficiency is essential. You'll lead discovery calls with CFOs, present to executive audiences, and produce written content in English. Our Financial Intelligence GTM is global—this isn't optional.

Strong Pluses
Experience with multi-entity consolidation (intercompany eliminations, currency conversion)
Familiarity with EPM/CPM tools (Adaptive, Anaplan, Jedox, OneStream)
Understanding of accounting principles and close processes
Experience in an advisory or consulting capacity
Nice to Have
Experience with ERP systems (NetSuite, SAP, Microsoft Dynamics)
German language skills (for DACH market expansion)
Previous sales or pre-sales exposureCzech language skills

You can look forward to the following benefits
Competitive salary + equity package.
Budget for education.
25 PTO's plus 3 sick days.
Cool new offices in the heart of Holesovice in Prague. You need to be 2-3 times a week in the office.
No set office hours and flexibility.

Keboola’s Core Values
Care, love and respect
No BS
Get your stuff done and do a bit more
Create and innovate
Learn!
One team

\n
Equal Opportunity Statement

Keboola is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive, equitable, and diverse workplace where everyone, regardless of race, colour, religion, gender, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or any other protected status, is given equal opportunity to thrive.
We value the unique perspectives and experiences of all employees and applicants, and we strive to ensure that all employment decisions are based on merit, qualifications, and business needs.

More About Keboola

Every day, Keboola connects data, systems, and people to do their best work all in one place. Keboola is a hybrid, multi-cloud data platform that reduces data complexity by streamlining and automating processes. Hundreds of innovative companies rely on Keboola to unlock the power of their data, bringing the potential of AI and advanced analytics to life. For more information, visit keboola.com or connect with us on LinkedIn, Twitter, and Facebook.

With Keboola, you can  build end to end data workflows, automate repetitive tasks, build and run data applications. All under one platform.

title: Head of SEO
description_text:
Join Hostinger, and we’ll grow fast! 🚀

We’re shaping the future of online success - powered by AI and driven by people. With 900+ talented professionals and over 4 million clients in 150 countries, we help creators and entrepreneurs bring their ideas to life faster and easier than ever before.

Our mission: To provide tools that help individuals and small businesses succeed online faster and easier.
Our culture: Guided by 10 company principles.
Our formula for success: Customer obsession, innovative products, and talented teams.

Your role at Hostinger

We’re looking for a Head of SEO who leads through strategy and leadership. Someone who can look at our SEO function and see ten opportunities for innovation. Someone who optimizes for business impact, not just rankings. Someone who makes the team better, our processes sharper, and our organic acquisition faster – all while questioning whether we're still doing things the right way.

You'll lead our SEO team, architect our AI-native search strategy, and ensure SEO is baked into product launches from day one. You'll work across organic channels (Content, YouTube, Localization) and partner with Product, Engineering, and Marketing teams to drive measurable new client acquisition and billings.

If you can build systems that enable both speed AND strategic innovation, coach teams to think like strategists, and constantly challenge "this is how we've always done it"? Even better.

Curious to learn more? Connect with your team: Emma Young, Head of Organic Marketing
\n

Your day-to-day
Develop and execute SEO strategy aligned with Hostinger's brand evolution
Own keyword strategy across all Hostinger content
Lead and develop your SEO team - coach them to think strategically, not just execute
Architect AI-native search strategies across all platforms
Build systems that bake SEO into launches from day one
Balance necessary work with strategic experiments and innovation
Constantly challenge current approaches 

Your skills and experience
5+ years of SEO strategy and execution with demonstrable growth results
Strategic content and technical SEO expertise
Keyword strategy ownership 
Competitive analysis and market intelligenceInternational/global SEO experience
Test-and-scale mindset- rapidly test, identify winners, scale efficiently
Analytics and data-driven decision making
AI-native search fluency - understands emerging platforms and can build strategies for evolving search landscape
Strategic leadership - proven ability to elevate teams and develop strategic thinking
Hiring and talent density focus
Business-first thinking - connects SEO decisions to new clients and billings
Understanding of tech products, or the ability to learn quickly, would be an advantage
Multi-channel thinking — seeing how one piece works across platforms 

Benefits for you
🚀 360 Growth: We provide limitless learning opportunities: access to platforms like Reforge and CoachHub, global conferences, physical and digital libraries, feedback culture, and mentoring through TesoXchange. Advance your career with internal mobility and grow with a team eager to share knowledge and support your success.
🎯 Freedom & responsibility: Work on your terms: from modern offices in Kaunas and Vilnius, the comfort of home, or anywhere in the world. Enjoy flexibility in managing your schedule and bring your ideas to life in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
💪Wellness simplified: Your health comes first with insurance from Day 1, gym memberships, recharge leave, Headspaces subscriptions, and regular health checks. Join our wellbeing-dedicated events, sports, arts, and hobby clubs, or simply enjoy the balance of a lifestyle that prioritizes wellness.
🎉 Work hard – play hard: Recognize hard work with company events like Summerfest & Winterfest, Town Hall, Meet the Client initiatives, team-buildings, and workations. Enjoy access to the Žalgiris Arena VIP Lounge and celebrate life’s big moments with milestone gifts for weddings, new parenthood, and graduations.

Compensation
Gross salary starting from 4400 EUR. Specific compensation is offered based on work experience, competence, and compliance with other job requirements. 

\n
Get ready to take your personal and professional growth to new heights! Join Hostinger today and be part of our journey 🚀
Three. Two. Onboard

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 5+ years of SEO strategy and execution experience with a focus on growth results. Expertise in strategic content and technical SEO, along with a test-and-scale mindset, is essential.

title: Sales Development Representative (Education / Student Loan Services)
description_text:
Sales Development Representative (Education / Student Loan Services)

Client Industry: Education / Student Loan Services
Client Location: California, USA
Assistant Schedule: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST

Role Summary
The client is an educational institution seeking to expand its current Virtual Assistant team by hiring additional support staff for student loan default management. The role focuses on outbound communication and account coordination to assist graduates who are in loan default and guide them back into active repayment or appropriate default resolution processes.

Goal
Support revenue generation through effective student loan default management and payment recovery, while maintaining consistent communication with graduates and internal processing teams.

Duties and Responsibilities include but are not limited to:
1. Outbound Student Outreach
Contact students and graduates who are currently in loan default to discuss account status and next steps.
2. Payment Recovery Calls
Make phone calls to encourage and guide borrowers toward resuming payments or entering approved repayment arrangements.
3. Account Coordination
Work closely with the default processing team to manage, update, and track student loan accounts.

Qualifications:
• Graduate of any bachelor's degree or certificate course.
• Proven Experience with Education / Student Loan Services
• Minimum of 1 year of sales experience and proven ability to meet and exceed sales quotas and successfully manage customer relationships
• Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal (at least C1 level)
• Excellent phone, email, and instant messaging communication skills
• Solid organizational and time management skills
• Strong interpersonal skills
• Tech savvy & familiar with current technologies, like desktop sharing, cloud services, CRM, and VoIP
• Proven experience with Go High Level (GHL)
• Knowledge of using AirCall (Power Dialer)
• Proactive & confident with keen attention to details
• Able to work on a graveyard shift

Software & Tools Required
• Phone system for outbound calls
• CRM or database system for managing student records and account notes

Technical Requirements:
• USB Headset with Noise Cancellation feature
• Working Webcam
• Computer with at least 1.8 GHz processor and at least 4GB RAM
• Main Internet Service Speed: at least 25 Mbps cable connection
• Backup Internet Service Speed: at least 10 Mbps

Benefits:
• Health Insurance (HMO) 
• Performance Incentives
• Job Security and Stability
• Paid Training
• Inclusive Culture
• Upskilling Opportunities
• 100% Work-From-Home
• Exceptionally Supportive Team
• Opportunities for Career Growth
• Fun Work Environment
• Holiday & Overtime Pay

Schedule: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM PST
Location: This is a remote job

Please note:
• Only qualified candidates will be invited to take the assessment & scheduled for an interview.
• We have other vacancies that might interest your friends & colleagues. They can check us out at our Jobs Website.
• You may also refer your friends using our Affiliate Marketing Program and earn up to $30 if your referral is hired.
\n

\n
₱45,000 - ₱50,000 a month

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: The Sales Development Representative will focus on outbound communication and account coordination to assist graduates in loan default. The role aims to support revenue generation through effective student loan default management and payment recovery.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a bachelor's degree or certificate and at least one year of sales experience in education or student loan services. Excellent communication skills in English and familiarity with relevant technologies are also required.

Hit "Learn More" to speak to an expert about transforming your business! 🚀

title: Production Controller
description_text:
ERG is a research and consulting firm that provides a wide range of support to federal, state, and commercial clients. ERG offers multidisciplinary teams with nationally recognized skills in engineering, science, economics, public health, informational technology, and communications. We hire people with the best minds and then provide them with a vibrant and flexible environment in which to develop their careers. The qualified individual must be highly motivated with the skills to prioritize, perform, and communicate effectively in a fast-paced environment. 

ERG is seeking a Production Controller (Planner/Scheduler) to provide on-site technical planning and scheduling support for maintenance and repair efforts at the Navy’s leading facility for ordnance, energetics, and explosive ordnance disposal solutions. The qualified individual must be motivated with the skills to prioritize, perform, and communicate effectively in a fast-paced environment. 

This position will be full time at the Naval Support Facility Indian Head and will include interaction with and support from ERG personnel at the facility, as well as from ERG’s offices in Fairfax and Arlington, Virginia. Indian Head is approximately 30 miles from Washington, D.C.; 25 miles from Alexandria, Virginia; and 60 miles from Fredericksburg, Virginia. Alternative work schedules may be considered. 
\n

Job Description:
Coordinate with technical department schedulers to plan for routine facility maintenance repair efforts to coincide with downtimes
Develop and maintain facility maintenance schedules using tools such as Microsoft Excel, Microsoft Project, or Primavera
Review upcoming maintenance work crew schedules to confirm plans and resource allocations are appropriate
Schedule and lead maintenance planning meetings
Resolve schedule conflicts and communicate changes
Track maintenance activities to completion and update the schedule accordingly

Qualifications and Skills:
Associate’s degree in facilities maintenance, engineering, or a related field is preferred
Relevant experience supporting facilities renovation and infrastructure projects
Ability to work independently and as part of a team
Flexibility to work after hours and on weekends as needed
Strong organizational and time management skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Ability to work independently and as part of a team
Ability to prioritize tasks and manage multiple projects simultaneously
Strong attention to detail and accuracy
This position will require a pre-employment physical exam
This position requires lifting objects weighing up to 50 pounds
Work may be performed in areas where potentially harmful physical and chemical agents are present (e.g., fumes, dust, heat, ionizing and non-ionizing radiation and chemicals)
You may be required to participate in medical surveillance programs
Must be able to stand, walk, push, pull, squat, bend, and reach during shifts
Must be able to use carts, dollies, hand trucks, and other gear to move items around
Must be able to go up and down stairs, where applicable
Must currently hold or be able to obtain a DoD Secret security clearance
Must be a U.S. citizen as this position is not eligible for Limited Access Authorizations

\n
$80,000 - $110,000 a year
\n
ERG offers competitive salaries and excellent benefits, including health and dental insurance, life insurance, long-term disability, educational benefits, FSAs, a generous 401k plan, profit sharing, an EAP, 11-20 paid vacation days per year, 10 paid holidays per year, 56 hours or more of sick leave (based on the state you work in) per year (pro-rated for part-time) and more. The salary range for all positions depends on the years and type of experience. 
ERG is an equal opportunity employer and complies with all applicable EEOC regulations. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual preference, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran. 
Please be aware, the only authentic corporate domain for ERG is https://www.erg.com. ERG may, on occasion, screen applicants via telephone or video interviews via Skype, Teams, GoToMeeting, or another type of video platform. However, any candidate extended a job offer might be asked to meet in person with an ERG employee before providing confidential personal information associated with new employment. 
If you’re a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you have the right to request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access ERG’s online application process as a result of your disability. To request accommodation, please contact Human Resources via email at Resumes-Lex@erg.com or call (781) 674-7293. 
ERG fosters a friendly, flexible work environment. ERGers are dedicated to serving clients who are committed to making the world a better place. We promote and recognize principles of fairness and respect in the work we do, the partnerships we foster, and the culture we value both within and outside of our organization.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Production Controller will coordinate with technical department schedulers to plan routine facility maintenance and develop maintenance schedules. They will also lead maintenance planning meetings and track maintenance activities to completion.
ai_requirements_summary: An associate's degree in facilities maintenance, engineering, or a related field is preferred, along with relevant experience in facilities renovation. Strong organizational skills and the ability to work independently or as part of a team are essential.
