title: Fraud Analyst  - London
description_text:
\n

\n
Fraud Analyst
London | Full - time | Hybrid | Shoreditch/Liverpool Street

Create the Future of Travel With Us
Whether it's visiting the people closest to us, starting an exciting adventure, or a career defining business trip, travel is essential. Yet we've all experienced the aches and pains of getting to our destination.

More than 4 billion airline passengers rely on technology that hasn't kept up with modern expectations. That's why we're rebuilding the infrastructure that underpins the travel industry simplifying systems and building tools that will make the future of travel effortless. Central to this mission is ensuring seamless, secure and reliable payment experiences, which are crucial for every transaction and a cornerstone of trust in our platform.

We were part of Y Combinator S18's cohort and backed by Benchmark, Blossom, Index Ventures, and Kima Ventures a fantastic set of investors that has helped build some of the world's largest companies.

Our London team is growing, and we're looking for an experienced Fraud Analyst join us on our journey.

The Role
We're looking for a curious and analytical Fraud Analyst to join our team. You'll analyse data, spot patterns, and write detection rules that help stop fraud in its tracks playing a vital role in protecting travellers businesses, and the integrity of our platform.

You'll work on complex, often ambiguous challenges in the ever ,evolving world of travel payments and bookings. If you love solving problems, diving into data, and making a real difference this is your chance.

What You'll Do:
• Identify fraud trends and develop a deep understanding of fraudulent behaviour on our platform through SQL, Google BigQuery, and Google Looker
• Formulate rules based on your analysis to mitigate fraudulent behaviour across our platform
• Carry out investigations of escalated cases related to potential misuse of our platform and assist with legal and customer enquiries
• Partner with our data science and engineering teams to advance our technological approaches to identifying fraudulent activity
• Work closely with engineering to enhance internal tools and databases that enable us to combat fraud and scale effectively
• Act as a key resource for other departments by providing analysis of customer and booking behaviour on the platform
• Present and communicate the results of analyses and investigations to stakeholders across the company

You'll Thrive Here If:
• You have experience in data analytics or a similar analytical role (through formal education or practical experience)
• You're comfortable working with and interpreting large datasets
• You have a knack for answering difficult questions using data and translating complex findings into clear, actionable reports
• You're proficient in writing queries and preparing reports using SQL or similar
• You're motivated by self ,learning and growing your skills when faced with new challenges
• You can manage multiple priorities effectively with a proactive, forward ,thinking mindset
Bonus: Prior knowledge of formulating rules to combat fraudulent behaviour or experience in travel/payments

What You'll Get:
•💰 Competitive salary + equity (you'll own part of Duffel)
• 🏝️ Work from anywhere policy
• 🛩️ Duffel travel allowance (use what we build)
• 🍼 Generous parental leave
• 🧘‍♂️ Sabbatical programme
• 🪩 Office in Shoreditch + regular team socials

Why Now Matters
As we scale to millions of bookings, fraud prevention becomes critical. You'll build the systems and rules that protect our platform from day one of this function, defining how we approach trust and security as we grow.

We're an equal opportunities employer. We believe that the key to our success is employing a diverse team, which is why recruitment decisions are only based on your experience and skills. We welcome applications from everyone ,regardless of age, sex, disability, sexual orientation, race, religion, or belief.

Note to recruitment agencies: Duffel does not accept speculative CVs from external parties. Any unsolicited CVs sent to us will be treated as property of Duffel, and any attached terms and conditions associated with these CVs will be null and void.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Fraud Analyst will analyze data to identify fraud trends and develop detection rules to mitigate fraudulent behavior. They will also investigate escalated cases and collaborate with data science and engineering teams to enhance fraud prevention measures.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have experience in data analytics or a similar analytical role and be comfortable working with large datasets. Proficiency in SQL and a proactive approach to problem-solving are also essential.

linkedin_org_description: The complete toolkit for selling travel online. Most of the world’s travel technology was built in a bygone era. Travel companies today need tools that are flexible enough to mould to customer expectations and powerful enough to support a growing business. Layer by layer, piece by piece, we’re rebuilding the infrastructure that underpins the travel industry to bring it into the modern era and usher in a new, effortless travel experience. We’re backed by Benchmark, Blossom, Index Ventures, Kima Ventures, and Y Combinator (S18).

title: Paid Ads Specialist
description_text:
We’re looking for a Paid Ads Specialist to support and execute performance marketing campaigns for education and training businesses focused on student enrollment growth. This role is hands-on and execution-driven, ideal for someone early to mid-career who has agency experience and wants to deepen their skills across Google, Meta, and TikTok ads.
You will start by supporting senior advertisers and managing smaller ad accounts, with a clear path toward owning campaigns end to end. This role is well suited for someone who understands paid ads fundamentals, communicates clearly, and is eager to grow into more responsibility.
If you enjoy performance marketing, care about measurable outcomes, and want to learn how enrollment-focused funnels actually work, this role is a strong fit.
Why You’ll Want to Join
You will be paid in USD bi-monthly every 15th and 30th
Up to 14 days of Paid Time Off annually starting Day 1
Observance of Holidays per company guidelines
100% remote setup so you can work wherever you’re most productive
Hands-on training and mentorship from experienced paid media strategists
Clear growth path from support role to independent account ownership
What You’ll Work On
Paid Ads Execution and Support
Support active ad accounts across Google Ads, Meta, and TikTok
Help manage campaigns with monthly budgets ranging from 3K to 15K USD
Assist with campaign setup, ad creation, budget pacing, and optimizations
Implement changes based on performance data and advertiser guidance
Account Management and Optimization
Monitor daily performance metrics and flag issues or opportunities
Assist with testing creatives, audiences, and campaign structures
Help maintain clean account structure and accurate tracking
Contribute to ongoing optimization of enrollment-focused funnels
Collaboration and Communication
Work closely with senior advertisers and a US-based team
Communicate clearly in written and spoken English during internal discussions
Support reporting and performance summaries for internal review
Learn how to translate client goals into practical ad execution
What You Bring
2 to 3 years of hands-on experience running paid ads, ideally in an agency environment
Experience working with Google Ads and Meta Ads required
Exposure to TikTok Ads is a strong plus
Strong written and verbal English communication skills
Comfortable starting in a support role and growing into more ownership
Detail-oriented with a performance mindset
Curious, coachable, and motivated to improve as a paid media operator
Nice to Have
Experience working with lead generation or education-focused campaigns
Familiarity with tracking, conversion events, and basic funnel concepts
Experience collaborating with US-based teams or clients
Comfort using reporting dashboards or spreadsheets for performance analysis
How to Apply
Please include:
Your updated resume
A short 1 to 2 minute Loom video introducing yourself and explaining your paid ads experience and what accounts or platforms you’ve worked on
Only candidates who submit a Loom video will be considered.
If you’re early in your paid media career, have solid agency fundamentals, and want to grow into a performance marketer who understands real enrollment outcomes, this role offers structure, mentorship, and room to level up fast.
Application Process Overview
Our comprehensive selection process ensures we find the right fit for both you and our clients:
Initial Application - Submit your application and complete our prequalifying questions
Video Introduction - Record an video introduction to showcase your communication skills and work experience
Role-Specific Assessment - Complete a homework assignment tailored to the position (if applicable)
Recruitment Interview - Initial screening with our talent team
Executive Interview - Meet with senior leadership to discuss role alignment
Client Interview - Final interview with the client team you'd be supporting
Background & Reference Check - Professional reference verification
Job Offer - Successful candidates receive a formal offer to join the team
Each stage is designed to evaluate your fit for the role while giving you insights into our company culture and expectations. We'll keep you informed throughout the process and provide feedback at each step.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Paid Ads Specialist will support and execute performance marketing campaigns, focusing on student enrollment growth. Responsibilities include managing ad accounts, assisting with campaign setup, and optimizing performance based on data.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 2 to 3 years of hands-on experience running paid ads, preferably in an agency environment. Experience with Google Ads and Meta Ads is required, while exposure to TikTok Ads is a plus.

title: Systems Administrator
locations_derived: Bogota, Capital District, RAP (Especial) Central, Colombia
description_text:
Job Title: System Administrator
About the Role: The OneStream System Administrator is responsible for managing, maintaining, and optimizing the OneStream platform.
Key Responsibilities:
• User access management: Ensure users have appropriate system access.
• System configuration: Set up and configure financial systems.
• Troubleshooting: Identify and resolve system issues.
• Data integration: Facilitate seamless data integration from various sources.
• Financial reporting: Create, maintain, and update financial reports and forms.
• Training and support: Provide training and support to users.
• Compliance: Ensure compliance with U.S. GAAP and internal financial controls.
• System upgrades: Coordinate and execute system upgrades.
• Documentation: Develop and maintain documentation for key processes and controls.
• Collaboration: Work with finance and IT teams to implement updates and enhance system performance.
Skills and Qualifications:
• Strong project management skills.
• Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.
• Ability to work effectively under pressure.
• Experience and knowledge of U.S. GAAP and/or Hyperion, OneStream, or similar ERP systems.

#Solvo

\n

\n
$1,999 - $2,000 a month
\n

title: SR Full Stack TypeScript
counties_derived: Região Integrada de Desenvolvimento do Distrito Federal e Entorno
locations_derived: Porto Alegre, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil
description_text:
About Coderio

Coderio designs and delivers scalable digital solutions for global companies. With a strong technical foundation and a product-oriented mindset, our teams lead complex software projects from architecture to execution. We value autonomy, clear communication, and technical excellence. We work closely with international teams and partners, building technology that creates real impact.
🌍 More information: http://coderio.com

We are seeking a backend specialist to own the technical foundation of our platform. You will architect scalable data structures, harden and secure core systems, and build the resilient backend engines that power our LLM agents at enterprise scale. If you're looking to move beyond simple API development and help architect the “brain” and “muscle” of a high-volume AI platform, this role is for you.

What You Can Expect (Responsibilities)

Design and optimize PostgreSQL + pgvector data models for high-volume, low-latency operations.
Architect secure, production-grade backend engines that integrate with LLMs and agent frameworks.
Implement system hardening, including authentication (RBAC), encryption, and secure API patterns.
Build scalable backend flows and APIs for cloud/serverless environments (AWS/GCP/Vercel).
Monitor and optimize AI model performance, debugging issues in distributed systems.
Collaborate with founders and data teams to define long-term architecture around intelligence and memory.

Requirements

7+ years in backend or full-stack engineering with a strong backend focus.
3+ years working with PostgreSQL, including performance tuning and data modeling.
4+ years combined expertise in TypeScript and Python for backend logic and AI/data pipelines.
2+ years in data-heavy or security-critical environments, with proven optimization and hardening experience.
3+ years deploying applications in cloud/serverless environments (AWS, GCP, Vercel, Fly.io).
1+ year working with LLM or agent orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, AutoGen, ReAct-style agents).
Demonstrated experience implementing Auth, RBAC, and secure API design.

Nice to Have

Experience with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, or Qdrant).
Redis expertise for caching, queues, or pub-sub patterns.
Familiarity with modern ORMs or API frameworks (Prisma, Drizzle, GraphQL, tRPC).
Exposure to compliance frameworks such as SOC2, GDPR, or HIPAA.


Benefits

100% remote
Long-term commitment with autonomy and impact
Strategic, high-visibility role within a modern engineering culture
Collaborative international team and strong technical leadership
Career development and growth opportunities within Coderio

Why Join Coderio?

At Coderio, we value talent regardless of location. We are a remote-first company, passionate about technology, collaboration, and fair compensation. We provide an inclusive and challenging environment with real opportunities for growth. If building impactful solutions motivates you, we’re waiting for you.
Apply now.
\n

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: You will design and optimize data models for high-volume operations and architect secure backend engines. Additionally, you will implement system hardening and collaborate with teams to define long-term architecture.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have over 7 years of experience in backend or full-stack engineering with a strong backend focus. Proficiency in PostgreSQL, TypeScript, Python, and experience in cloud environments is also required.

title: Data Scientist - Music Mission
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
We are seeking a Data Scientist to join Spotify’s Artist-First AI Music Lab.

Our team pioneers state-of-the-art generative technologies for music that create breakthrough experiences for fans and artists. We invent entirely new products that center and celebrate artists and creatives—putting artists and songwriters first through principles of choice, transparency, fair compensation, and deepened artist–fan connection.

As a Data Scientist on this team, you’ll help bridge cutting edge AI research with outstanding product experiences. Your work will ensure that every feature we launch is grounded in data-driven insight and meaningfully strengthens the connection between more than 700 million listeners and the creators they love.

For more information, see this press release: https://newsroom.spotify.com/2025-10-16/artist-first-ai-music-spotify-collaboration/
\n

What You'll Do
Own analytical projects end-to-end, from hypothesis generation and data exploration to recommendations for product leadership
Develop and own success metrics for generative music features and systems
Design and analyze A/B tests and causal studies to evaluate product and model impact
Perform exploratory analyses to uncover opportunities that improve experiences for listeners and artists
Build scalable dashboards to monitor feature health and ecosystem impact
Design and run evaluations for generative music systems, assessing risks and opportunities across prompts, outputs, and quality
Collaborate with Product, Design, Research, Marketing, and Engineering to translate insights into product requirements
Partner closely with Engineers and AI Researchers to integrate evaluation signals into model development workflows
Communicate complex findings through clear, actionable narratives that inform product strategy and roadmap decisions

Who You Are
You have a degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Economics, Operations Research, quantitative social science, or a related field (or equivalent experience)
You bring 4+ years of experience as a Data Scientist influencing product decisions through data
You’re highly proficient in SQL and Python and comfortable working with large-scale datasets
You use AI-powered tools (e.g., Cursor, Copilot) to accelerate analysis and workflows
You have strong product intuition and a results-focused perspective— you seek the “why” behind the data
You have experience with A/B testing, causal inference and advanced statistical methods, and exercise strong judgment in methodological choices
You understand machine learning systems and can evaluate models beyond offline metrics, applying human judgment to quality and impact
You thrive in ambiguous, zero-to-one environments and enjoy defining metrics & opportunities for entirely new product categories
You’re motivated by creating real value for music fans and music creators

Where You'll Be
We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, you can be within the EST timezone region as long as we have a work location. 
This team operates within the Eastern Standard time zone for collaboration

\n
The United States base range for this position is $110,018 - $157,169 plus equity. The benefits available for this position include health insurance, six month paid parental leave, 401(k) retirement plan, monthly meal allowance, 23 paid days off, 13 paid flexible holidays, paid sick leave. These ranges may be modified in the future.

Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.

At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.

Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.
ai_remote_location: New York, New York, United States

title: Coordonnateur.trice de formation
description_text:
On te reconnaît pour ton remarquable sens de l’organisation et ta capacité à gérer simultanément plusieurs priorités? Ton approche client, ta rigueur et ton souci d’optimisation font de toi un·e professionnel·le solide et fiable?
Si oui, tu pourrais être la personne idéale pour ce poste.

Qui sommes-nous?

Chez AFI par Edgenda, nous sommes spécialisés dans la stratégie, le développement et la diffusion de programmes de développement des talents. Nous sommes actuellement le chef de file dans le développement des compétences au Canada et le seul à offrir une offre transversale dans des espaces réinventés.

Chez AFI, nous évoluons dans un environnement stimulant, en constante effervescence, où chaque journée apporte son lot de défis et d’occasions de briller. En tant que Coordonnateur·trice à la formation, tu seras au cœur de notre succès opérationnel en veillant à ce que nos services de formation soient impeccables, tant pour nos équipes que pour notre clientèle. Tu seras le point de contact clé, alliant efficacité et attention aux détails, tout en jouant un rôle actif dans nos projets stratégiques.
\n

Les avantages à nous rejoindre
Pour ton bien-être: Un ensemble d’assurances collectives y compris un service de télémédecine, une allocation de dépenses, puis des programmes REER et RPDB pour planifier ton avenir.
Pour célébrer tes succès: Un salaire avec bonus annuel fondé sur tes objectifs professionnels.
Pour apprendre tous les jours: Un montant annuel de 2500$ alloué à ton développement professionnel, en plus d’un écosystème de collègues humains détenant des bagages variés et de programmes te permettant de voyager et de travailler à l’étranger.
Pour un quotidien sans tracas: Un horaire de travail flexible parce que nous avons la conciliation travail-vie personnelle à cœur.
Pour profiter de temps de qualité: Des vacances annuelles et des petits congés pour les évènements spéciaux de la vie.
Pour avoir du plaisir avec tes collègues: Des espaces de bureaux modernes et vivants dans lesquels une communauté active et un club social se surpassent continuellement afin de te garder actif et connecté.

Plus concrètement, voici comment tu pourras t’impliquer
Représenter notre service client en assurant une expérience exceptionnelle en tout temps.
Accueillir avec enthousiasme les visiteurs et participants à nos formations.
Maintenir à jour notre offre de formation sur les différentes plateformes et assurer un contrôle qualité.
Coordonner la logistique complète des formations (horaires, salles, équipements).
Gérer les contrats, ententes et factures liées à nos services.
Collaborer avec le Coordonnateur Évènements et Communications internes à la planification d’évènements mémorables.
Gérer les priorités et trouver des solutions créatives aux imprévus avec débrouillardise et autonomie.
Participer à la réalisation des projets en compagnie des différentes équipes.
Assurer la qualité de nos environnements de formation, qu’ils soient physiques ou virtuels.
Piloter les suivis clients et analyser les sondages de satisfaction pour toujours viser l’amélioration.
Mettre à jour nos processus, tout en proposant des pistes d’innovation.

Tu as le profil que l’on recherche ?
Tu détiens un diplôme de 1er cycle dans un domaine pertinent.
Tu possèdes 2 à 5 ans d’expérience dans un poste de coordination, logistique, évènementiel et/ou service à la clientèle.
Tu as d’excellentes aptitudes de communication en français et en anglais (écrit et oral), car le poste implique d’accompagner des clients dans les deux langues.
Tu es disponible pour assurer la vigie de nos bureaux de Québec sur place tous les jours de la semaine.
Microsoft 365 n’a plus aucun secret pour toi.
Tu fais preuve de curiosité et d’aisance avec les technologies et apprends rapidement à utiliser de nouveaux outils.

Et si en plus, on dit de toi que :
Tu te démarques par ta communication hors pair et ton esprit de synthèse.
Tu fais preuve de rigueur, d'autonomie et de polyvalence dans tout ce que tu entreprends.
Ton service à la clientèle est irréprochable et ta flexibilité à toute épreuve.
Tu planifies avec brio ton temps et tu gères aisément les priorités qui en découlent.
Tu fais preuve de créativité et d’innovation.

\n
Alors on a hâte de découvrir ton potentiel!

Si tu te reconnais dans cette description, n’hésite pas à nous faire parvenir ta candidature.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Coordinator of Training will ensure impeccable training services for both teams and clients, acting as a key contact point. Responsibilities include coordinating logistics, managing contracts, and ensuring quality in training environments.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a bachelor's degree and 2 to 5 years of experience in coordination, logistics, or customer service. Excellent communication skills in both French and English are required, along with proficiency in Microsoft 365.

Go RH se démarque par sa créativité, son authenticité et la pertinence de ses interventions, que ce soit pour des besoins reliés au recrutement, aux enjeux liés à la réorganisation d’une entreprise ou, tout simplement, pour structurer la gestion des ressources humaines dans une organisation où tout est à faire, son équipe de professionnel.le.s RH sait s’adapter à toutes les situations et contribue au succès des entreprises, aux quatre coins du Québec. 


En 2022, l’entreprise a fusionné avec la firme du Bas-Saint-Laurent PBRH, propulsant ainsi la croissance de Go RH à un niveau supérieur.

title: Sales Operations Specialist - Incentive & Performance
description_text:
🪐 Discover our galaxy

Join the Future of Work ! Malt is Europe's leading freelance marketplace, connecting over 900,000 talented freelancers with 90,000+ companies. Founded in 2013, we're transforming how work gets done through our tech-powered, human-centered platform. What makes us different:

- A diverse team of 600 Malters across 6 European countries
- A culture that champions equality (50% of our Comex are women) and inclusive growth
- Backed by top investors including ISAI, Serena Capital, Eurazeo Growth, Goldman Sachs, and BPI
- A mission to give everyone the freedom to work differently

Ready to help shape the future of work? Your next chapter starts here! 🪐

At Malt we believe that Ambition is the Way, so all lists of missions and responsibilities are non-exhaustive.

Explore your future career 🔭 

As our Incentive & Performance Specialist, you are the guardian of Malt’s commercial "behavioral engine." You translate our business strategy into clear, motivating pay plans and ensure they are perfectly executed. You are the point of truth for how our 170+ commercial Malters are rewarded for their impact.
\n

Key responsibilities ✨
Compensation Design & Execution (25%): Lead the design and reviews of commercial team pay plans and own the "official playbook". You ensure the plans drive the right behaviors for the business.
System Implementation (20%): partner with our Sales Stack & Salesforce Product Managers as well as Data & FP&A Teams to ensure these plans are properly implemented within our internal systems (Salesforce, Looker, Amalia, Pigment). You ensure a seamless flow from a "Closed Won" deal in the CRM to a commission check.
Target Setting & Quota Management (25%): Drive the half-yearly target setting process & tools, working closely with Data, FP&A, and Commercial leads.
Performance Auditing (30%): Monitor individual performance (IC) to provide actionable insights. Back up the Planning & Performance owner on recurring and adhoc performance analysis. Support FP&A on quarterly achievement validations.

About You 🧑‍🚀
Background: 5+ years in Sales Strategy, Revenue Operations, or Management Consulting.
The "Why" over the "How": You care about the behavioral impact of a commission accelerator more than the technical configuration of the tool.
Influence: You are a strong communicator who can present performance insights to the Executive team and explain complex pay logic to the field teams.
Technical Stack: Advanced mastery of Excel/Google Sheets is a must. Experience with Looker, Amalia, Pigment, or Salesforce is highly preferred.
Stakeholder Partner: You are comfortable partnering with C&B (Compensation & Benefits) and FP&A to align incentives with the company budget.

How to join the mission? 🚀
First call with Jeanne Vinot Préfontaine, our Talent Acquisition Specialist, to better understand your background, aspirations and answer any questions you may have. (30-45 minutes)
Interview with Matthieu Chaignot Sales Operations Director, to dive into the role and ensure it’s a match! (45-60 minutes)
Business case for you to complete and present to Matthieu and Louise Chomel in order to assess your skills for the role and presentation skills. 
Final interview with Edith Savoy to discuss Malt’s long-term vision. 

\n
Malt is the perfect space to thrive personally and professionally 💫

- Onboarding: Before easing into your new role, you’ll spend your first week learning about our culture, products, and services with other new joiners at our office in Paris
- Equity: Every Malter is entitled to stock options 
- Lunch vouchers (legal benefit): €9/day on your Swile card
- Transportation (legal benefit): 50% reimbursement (any type of transport)
- Healthcare insurance (legal benefit) 
- RTT: From 8 to 12 days off per year
- Sports: Company participation to a Gymlib (Wellpass) membership
- Malt sabbatical: Every three years, all Malters are entitled to a one month fully paid sabbatical leave
- Free books: If you’re interested in learning more about any topic relevant to Malt’s business, just tell us the books you’d like to read, and we’ll order them for you—without any questions asked or approval processes to follow
- Remote work: Hybrid policy balancing on-site and home office
- Team building events organized every year

Ready? Get your ticket to Malt 🪐 

At Malt, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We recruit based on skills, experience, and potential, without any form of discrimination related to age, gender, sexual orientation, ethnicity, religion, or disability. Our mission is to create a work environment where everyone feels valued, respected, and safe to thrive.

Your profile may be subject to background screening. For more information see our candidate privacy policy.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Incentive & Performance Specialist is responsible for designing and executing pay plans for the commercial team, ensuring they align with business strategies. They also monitor individual performance and provide insights to support decision-making.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have over 5 years of experience in Sales Strategy, Revenue Operations, or Management Consulting, with a focus on the behavioral impact of compensation plans. Strong communication skills and proficiency in Excel and Salesforce are essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: The leading freelance marketplace in Europe & the Middle East 👋 For help, contact us at support@malt.com.
linkedin_org_locations: Raamplein 1, Amsterdam, North Holland 1016 XK, NL
linkedin_org_description:
Malt is a platform that connects businesses with freelancers, finding the perfect match for every project.

Our mission is to provide everyone with the freedom to choose what work they do and who they do it with. By helping to redefine the world of work and the relationships we have with it, we hope to empower everyone to shape their careers to fit exactly what they’re looking for.

Malt was founded in 2013 by Vincent Huguet and Hugo Lassiège. 

In March 2022, Malt acquired Comatch, enabling us to create a dedicated category for independent management consultants and interim executives where clients can access vetted expertise, known as Malt Strategy. 

We have now gained the trust of more than 850,000+ freelancers and 90,000+ clients, becoming one of the leading marketplaces for freelance talent. We are currently operational in nine countries and regions: Belgium, France, Germany, the Netherlands, the Nordics, Spain, Switzerland, the UAE and the UK.

Malt has evolved into a successful scaleup with more than 700 employees from 44 different nationalities who share the same values of care, ambition, joy, and autonomy.

Join us and become part of the future of work!

title: Analyst - R01560500
description_text:
Analyst
\n

Primary Skills
SQL, Advanced Excel, Power BI, SSIS, Power App 

Specialization
Power BI Development: BI Specialist 

Job requirements
Job Title: MS Power BI Developer Job Description: We are seeking a skilled MS Power BI Developer to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will be responsible for developing and implementing business intelligence solutions using Microsoft Power BI. You will collaborate closely with business analysts and stakeholders to understand their requirements and translate them into technical solutions that leverage Power BI's capabilities. Your primary focus will be on designing, developing, and maintaining BI dashboards, reports, and data visualizations that provide actionable insights. Responsibilities: Develop Power BI reports, dashboards, and KPI scorecards based on business requirements. Design, implement, and optimize data models and ETL processes to support BI initiatives. Collaborate with business users to gather and analyze requirements, prioritize tasks, and deliver BI solutions. Ensure data accuracy and integrity in Power BI solutions by implementing quality assurance best practices. Create documentation and provide training to end-users on the use of Power BI reports and dashboards. Stay current with industry trends and best practices in data visualization and BI technologies. Requirements: Proven experience (4-6 Years) as a Power BI Developer or similar role. Proficient in Microsoft Power BI (Power Query, DAX, Power BI Desktop, Power BI Service). Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts, data modeling, and ETL processes. Experience with SQL databases (SQL Server, MySQL, etc.) and proficiency in writing complex SQL queries. Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and effectively communicate with stakeholders. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a keen attention to detail. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field (preferred). Preferred Qualifications: Microsoft certifications related to Power BI (e.g., DA-100: Analyzing Data with Power BI) would be a plus. Experience with other BI tools (Tableau, QlikView, etc.) is a bonus. 

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves developing and implementing business intelligence solutions primarily using Microsoft Power BI, focusing on designing, developing, and maintaining BI dashboards, reports, and data visualizations. Responsibilities include developing reports, optimizing data models and ETL processes, gathering requirements, ensuring data accuracy, and providing end-user training.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have proven experience, ideally 4-6 years, as a Power BI Developer, demonstrating proficiency in Microsoft Power BI tools like Power Query, DAX, and Power BI Service. Strong skills in SQL databases, data warehousing concepts, and ETL processes are required, along with excellent analytical and communication abilities.

title: Senior Privatrådgivare | SEB Arenastaden/Sollentuna
description_text:
Är du duktig på att bygga långsiktiga relationer? Tänker du alltid kund först och vill samtidigt bidra till SEBs affär genom helhetsrådgivning? Vi söker nu en Senior privatrådgivare till team SEB Arenastaden/Sollentuna.

SEB är en ledande nordeuropeisk finansiell koncern med internationellt perspektiv. Vi tillhandahåller finansiella tjänster för företag, institutioner och privatkunder. Vi vill bidra till hållbar tillväxt och göra skillnad för våra kunder, aktieägare, medarbetare och samhället i stort. Genom vår expertis och höga servicenivå skapar vi möjligheter för våra kunder att fatta välgrundade beslut så att de kan förverkliga sina affärsambitioner och mål. Nu och för framtiden.

Om rollen: 
Som Senior Privatrådgivare är du ansvarig för att vårda, utveckla och växa din egen kundstock. Du har ett eget ansvar för att fylla din kalender och jobbar proaktivt med helhetsrådgivning. Din utgångspunkt är i den lokala marknaden. I rollen ligger stort fokus på att prospektering och att ta in nya kunder till banken. Detta genom ett nära samarbete med våra företagsrådgivare såväl som med externa partners.

Vem vi letar efter: 
Vi tror att du som söker är en nyfiken person med ett genuint intresse för ekonomi och människor. Du är en lagspelare och även duktig på att skapa långsiktiga relationer med såväl kunder som kollegor. Du är lyhörd för kundens behov och det är självklart för dig att alltid tänka brett och erbjuda hela SEB till våra kunder. För att lyckas i uppdraget krävs ett stort affärsdriv, engagemang och god samarbetsförmåga. Du ser förändring som en möjlighet och vill vara med i den digitala omställning som SEB gör.
Rollen kräver SwedSec licens för Rådgivare inklusive certifiering försäkringsförmedling samt bolånelicens.

Vad vi erbjuder:
Vi erbjuder en spännande arbetsplats i Arenastaden med stora möjligheter att utvecklas i rollen genom interna och externa nätverk.

Att arbeta hos oss innebär att du med stor passion, nyfikenhet och drivkraft kommer att vara delaktig i en positiv och utvecklande miljö präglad av stort engagemang, kamratskap och professionella medarbetare som jobbar nära varandra för kundens bästa.

På SEB är vi övertygade om att inkludering och mångfald är avgörande för att driva tillväxt och innovation, leverera service i världsklass till våra kunder samt skapa värde för samhället i stort. En inkluderande organisation för oss innebär en kultur där vi är nyfikna på olika perspektiv och tankar, där alla kan bidra till sin fulla potential och känner sig uppskattade och respekterade. Mångfald på SEB betyder uppskattning och respekt för varandras skillnader och bakgrund. Alla är välkomna hos oss, oavsett ålder, kön, etnicitet, tro, sexuell läggning eller funktionshinder.

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!
Bifoga ditt CV och ett personligt brev där du beskriver dig själv och vad du kan bidra med. Eftersom vi väljer ut kandidater löpande får du gärna skicka in din ansökan idag men senast den 27 februari. Om du har frågor kring tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Privatmarknadschef Kazan Amin via kazan.amin@seb.se

SEB Sverige har en omplaceringsskyldighet, varför denna tjänst kan komma att tillsätts genom intern omplacering.
Observera att våra slutkandidater genomgår bakgrundskontroller. Denna process inkluderar exempelvis identitetskontroll, verifiering av kvalifikationer, kreditkontroller, företagsengagemang och kontroll av brottsregister. Även slumpmässiga drogtester kan genomföras.
\n

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Senior Privatrådgivare, you will be responsible for managing and growing your own client portfolio while proactively engaging in holistic advisory services. The role emphasizes prospecting and acquiring new clients through collaboration with corporate advisors and external partners.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate is curious, has a genuine interest in finance and people, and excels at building long-term relationships. A SwedSec license for advisors, including certification for insurance mediation and mortgage licensing, is required for this role.

title: Enterprise Account Executive
description_text:
We are Ataccama, and we are on a mission to power a better future with data. Our product enables both technical and less technical ‘data people’ across their organizations to create high-quality, governed, safe, and reusable data products. It’s what made us a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant® for Data Quality Solutions™, and what inspired Bain Capital Tech Opportunities to invest in our future growth.

Our vision is to be the leading AI-powered cloud data management company and to do that, we’re making Ataccama a great place to work and grow. Our people are located across the globe. They succeed by collaborating as a team and thrive in our company culture defined by these core values:

Challenging Fun
ONE Team
Customer Centric
Candid and Caring
Aim High
\n

Your Challenge
Identify and establish connections with new prospects.
Prepare proposals and responses to procurement requests.
Work independently to develop account plans.
Initiate direct contact and build strong relationships with customers at the Director, Vice President, and Senior Management levels.
Develop presentations in cooperation with our presales team, lead sales calls, and act as a trusted advisor to highlight the value of Ataccama solutions for client businesses.
Manage the sales pipeline and meet regularly with the regional CEO and global sales team to update sales forecasts.
Monitor and analyse industry segments to keep abreast of current and developing customer trends and requirements, and create tactical sales strategies.
Negotiate contracts.
Ambitiously pursue, achieve, and exceed your annual revenue target.

Is This You?
You have strong experience in B2B software sales (5+ years preferred, but not mandatory), ideally in data management.
You are comfortable with data management technology, ready to learn more, and know how to explain complex technical concepts to business users.
You have a hunter mindset, a can-do attitude, and a strong sense of accountability.
You are self-motivated and can produce results with minimal oversight.
You welcome an international work environment and you are willing to travel.
You are tech-savvy, efficient, and meticulous when it comes to follow-ups and maintaining sales contacts and pipeline records.
You are tenacious and diligent.
You’re not looking for just a job. You want long-term work that is challenging, fun, and rewarding.

\n
Perks & Benefits 
• Long-Term Incentive Program
• 5 sick days and 25 days of vacation, with the option to request additional Enhanced Time-Off days when needed
• The Global Family Support Program - a paid leave program to help all parents focus on the new addition to their family
• Pension plan 
• "Bring Your Friend" referral program
• Flexible working hours & hybrid work setup
• Health insurance provided by Vitality
• Online courses & company access to Udemy to hone your skills
• Conference tickets to the best industry events of the year
• Cycle to work scheme

Work equipment
• Company laptop
• Company mobile phone

At Ataccama, our core values are Candid & Caring, so we are upfront about our process and details that are important to you. We sometimes use AI tools to help us with things like reviewing applications, taking notes from screening conversations, scheduling interviews, or supporting assessments. These tools make the process smoother and fairer — but don’t worry, they never make the final decision. Every hiring decision is made by our Talent Acquisition Partners and Hiring Managers, with AI only acting as a helpful assistant. We believe technology should support the process, not replace the human touch. 

We currently use AI-assisted tools - Metaview for interview notes and Lever Talent Fit to help highlight key experience.

While we highly value cooperation with all our business partners, we don’t accept unsolicited resumes from any sources other than directly from a candidate. We reserve the right not to pay any fee for sending an unsolicited offer containing the details or resume of a job candidate, even if the relevant candidate is employed by our company.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves identifying new prospects, preparing proposals, developing account plans independently, and building strong relationships with senior-level customers. Responsibilities also include leading sales calls, managing the sales pipeline, updating forecasts, and negotiating contracts to achieve annual revenue targets.

Essential attributes include a hunter mindset, self-motivation, accountability, willingness to travel internationally, and meticulous record-keeping.

title: Senior Solution Developer in Finance Tribe | SEB, Vilnius
description_text:
Do you want to create innovative solutions in a global environment and collaborate with experts to meet business needs?

With technology as our driving force, we provide world-class services to SEB group customers and business divisions in 20 sites worldwide. With us, you'll be part of a dedicated team of over 2800 employees who constantly work towards improving business development and growth. Do you want to work at the interface between ground-breaking technology and a world of financial flows? Welcome to SEB Global Services! 

About the role:
As a Senior Solution Developer you will be joining an ambitious, self-organized and curious team within Data
Transformation & Process Automation Unit – responsible for development, maintenance and transformation of several applications. Together with the other team member developers you will contribute to the design and development of internal tools—including APIs, microservices, web applications, and cloud-native products— required to support important daily flows and other teams.
This role offers the opportunity to take ownership of new functionality and projects, influence architectural decisions, collaborate with business analysts and architects to deliver production-ready solutions.

Our tech stack includes: 
.NET 9+, EF Core, MS SQL, Kafka, Angular, GCP, Terraform, Docker, OpenShift, Git, Azure DevOps & GitHub Actions, – those are the enablers for a successful delivery of needed application and will help you to grow in different ways.

Our diverse tech stack allows flexibility and creative freedom, promotes collaboration through knowledge sharing culture.
\n

To thrive in this role, we believe you have:
Working experience with C#, .NET, Entity Framework Core, MS SQL
Experience in API, Web application development, microservices architecture
Proficiency in at least one JavaScript framework (Angular, React, or Vue)
Knowledge in building CI/CD pipelines using YAML (GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps, GitLab CI)
Knowledge about Cloud-native solutions - not limited to hosting applications in the Cloud, but actively leveraging cloud resources and tools such as Cloud Functions, Storage, BigQuery, Pub/Sub, Workflows
Familiarity with Kafka, Python, Microsoft Entra, Hangfire
Analytical skills to handle complex information
Great communication skills and fluency in English

\n
€3,800 - €5,500 a month
The above indicated range is the monthly salary range for this position before tax deduction. The final offer will depend on the experience and competencies of the selected candidate. Overall remuneration package consists of the salary together with other benefits.
\n
Do you want to be a part of this team?

Welcome to our inclusive culture, where our shared values inspire and uplift our team. We celebrate diversity and strive to ensure every employee feels seen, heard, and valued. 

We are looking forward to your application by February 27, 2026
Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application before the deadline. If you have questions about the position, please contact Erika Samuolė – erika.samuolė@seb.se 

Learn more about working at SEB here: Technology | SEB

It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.

title: [OZ Forensics] Pre-sales Engineer
description_text:
A Unico é a maior rede de verificação de identidade do mundo e um pilar de confiança na sociedade digital. Com soluções baseadas em biometria facial, machine learning e camadas reforçadas de segurança, autenticamos com 100% de certeza quem está realizando uma transação e os riscos de identidade atrelados. Desta forma, nossas soluções combatem fraudes, protegem dados e promovem a confiança entre pessoas, empresas e governos, colaborando para a construção de um mundo mais seguro e menos burocrático.
Para chegar lá, investimos acima de tudo nas nossas pessoas, que formam um time incrível e comprometido com esse grande objetivo. E aí, vem com a gente? 

Nossa cultura
Nossos valores são a base para tudo que fazemos! Aqui na Unico, agimos sem fronteiras; impactamos sem rodeios; somos transparentes mesmo em situações difíceis; resilientes e adaptáveis para evoluir; fazemos a coisa certa do jeito certo e somos ousados ao liderar a construção do futuro. Nosso ambiente de trabalho é descontraído e colaborativo, cheio de oportunidades de aprendizado e possibilidades para você desenvolver sua carreira com autonomia e apoio de líderes e colegas.

Support the sales cycle by owning the technical pre-sales process: discovery,
solution design, demos, PoCs, and technical risk management. Ensure a smooth
handover to implementation/onboarding teams by providing an integration plan,
technical documentation, and clearly stated assumptions and constraints.
Note:this role includes technical coordination of onboarding and handover; it is
not a full-time implementation/delivery role
\n

Key responsibilities
Lead technical discovery: understand customer architecture, constraints
(security, privacy, latency, scale), and success criteria.
Design solution architecture for SaaS and On-Prem deployments, including
integration paths for Web SDK / Mobile SDK and API usage.
Deliver technical demos and presentations tailored to customer use cases
and risk profile.
define scope, datasets/assumptions, evaluation metrics, timeline, and
acceptance criteria.
liveness/PAD/IAD threats and mitigations
fraud vs conversion trade-offs (thresholds/metrics)performance/scaling (latency p95/p99, throughput, rate limits) Build pre-sales assets: reference architectures, integration checklists, demo
scripts, FAQ, competitor positioning (fact-based).
Ensure a clean handover to onboarding/implementation: architecture
diagram, integration checklist, operational requirements, and risk register.
Stay current with industry trends in biometrics, liveness attacks, and anti-
fraud techniques.d

Technical skills
Solid understanding of SaaS and On-Prem architectures and operations:
authentication/authorization basics (API keys/OAuth/JWT concepts)
logging/monitoring, SLAs/SLOs, incident basics
scalability concepts: latency, throughput, concurrency, rate limiting
high availability / disaster recovery concepts
Integration skills:
API-first integrations; ability to reason about client-side (mobile/web) + backend flows
basic familiarity with Docker and containerized deployments;
Kubernetes familiarity is a strong advantage
Liveness/PAD competence:
can explain common attack vectors
(presentation/replay/injection/deepfake etc.) and practical mitigation
approaches
ounderstands FAR/FRR and threshold trade-offs; can discuss how to
evaluate impact on conversion and fraud risk
Strong written and verbal communication: ability to translate technical
constraints into business outcomes and manage expectations honestly

Nice-to-have
Prior experience with biometrics, IDV/KYC, anti-fraud, face recognition, or
liveness vendors.
Experience with cloud Marketplaces (especially AWS Marketplace, cloud
procurement, multi-region considerations).
Experience with enterprise On-Prem deployments (air-gapped/limited
connectivity, proxies, whitelisting, compliance constraints)

3–5+ years in a pre-sales / solutions engineer / technical consultant role for a
B2B product with APIs and/or SDKs.
Experience leading discovery, demos, and PoCs with enterprise customersand multiple stakeholders.
Portuguese: Native/Fluent
English: Fluent
Spanish: nice to have

\n
Conheça os benefícios de se tornar um Ser Unico

Benefício flexível: R$1.500,00 por mês que podem ser distribuídos nas modalidades VA/VR, Cultura, Mobilidade e Flex Férias;
Auxílio home office: valor mensal de R$ 120,00 para pagamento de contas e consumo;
PLR: elegível a Participação nos Lucros e Resultados conforme acordo sindical;
Benefício Educação: a Unico financia 100% dos estudos para 100% dos colaboradores através de cursos disponíveis na plataforma Unico Skill. Você pode escolher entre graduação, pós-graduação, MBAs, idiomas, cursos especializantes e mentorias;
Bem-estar: acesso à plataformas como Gympass e Totalpass;
Licença parental estendida: 180 dias para gestantes e cuidadores principais do processo de adoção e 60 dias para não gestantes e cuidados secundários do processo de adoção, com salário e benefícios pagos integralmente ao longo de todo o período.

Local de trabalho

Adotamos o modelo de trabalho que prioriza a conexão e engajamento entre os Seres, o nosso #conectaunico, modelo de trabalho híbrido (2x por semana presencial).

E mais…

Oferecemos um plano de carreira estruturado de acordo com suas competências e interesses, com oportunidade de crescimento como especialista ou líder, incentivando o seu desenvolvimento pessoal e profissional através de ciclos de avaliação de performance semestrais (com feedback estruturado e plano de desenvolvimento individual);
Para completar a sua experiência, por aqui temos palestras, meetups, workshops, treinamentos, day off no aniversário, reunioff às quartas-feiras, festas e celebrações internas/externas, happy hour presencial quinzenal, grupos de afinidade e ginástica laboral.

Diversidade & Inclusão

Na Unico estamos empenhados em construir uma cultura inclusiva, valorizando em todas as nossas posições a candidatura de todas as pessoas, independentemente de sua etnia, gênero, orientação sexual, política, religião, deficiência, naturalidade ou qualquer outro tipo de característica.
Estamos construindo um ambiente de trabalho inclusivo e para nos apoiar em melhores práticas, criamos o formulário de Diversidade & Inclusão. Te convidamos a respondê-lo pra nos ajudar nesse processo, mas deixamos claro que você pode optar por não fazer isso sem impacto na sua avaliação. O compartilhamento desses dados não irá afetar o seu desempenho no processo seletivo e será usado somente para fins de criação de estratégias de inclusão.

Quer conhecer mais sobre a Unico?

Assista nosso manifesto;
Acompanhe nossas redes sociais: LinkedIn e Instagram

Reconheço que, antes de me candidatar, li o Aviso de Privacidade aos candidatos, que está disponível em: Aviso de privacidade para a pessoa candidata Unico | Privacy - Uso Interno

title: BI Report Developer
locations_derived: Jersey City, New Jersey, United States
description_text:
Join a National Top Workplace 
Named a Top Workplace in the USA and Top Remote Workplace, Kobie is where the best minds in loyalty come together, driven by passion and innovation. We’re always looking for talented individuals who are ready to join a collaborative, growth-focused culture. As a partner to some of the world’s most recognized brands, we are leaders in loyalty, helping brands build lasting emotional connections with their consumers. 
Join Us from Anywhere 
While our headquarters are nestled in sunny St. Petersburg, Florida, Kobie embraces a flexible work environment, offering teammates the freedom to work remotely. We understand the importance of work-life balance and support our team with: 

· Flexible Time Off to recharge when needed 
· Nine Company-Wide Holidays 
· A diverse suite of benefits prioritizing your growth, development, and personal well-being 

Discover more about our perks and benefits here. 
Kobie is a values-led organization where we believe that everyone is a leader, regardless of their position or role. 


About the team and what we’ll build together

Join a collaborative and forward-thinking Data & Analytics team that’s passionate about transforming data into actionable insights. As a Business Intelligence Report Developer, you’ll play a key role in shaping the reporting landscape for multiple large-scale clients. Together, we’ll build scalable, high-quality BI solutions that drive strategic decisions and elevate client experiences. You’ll work closely with data engineers, analysts, and business stakeholders to deliver reporting tools that are not only accurate and efficient, but also intuitive and impactful. This is a great opportunity to contribute to a growing BI ecosystem that values innovation, quality, and continuous improvement.
\n

How you will make an impact 
Design, develop, and maintain reports and dashboards using SSRS and Sigma Analytics.
Write and optimize SQL queries to support reporting applications in a complex relational database environment.
Collaborate with the data warehouse team to identify data gaps and opportunities for enhancement.
Create clear, user-friendly documentation for BI tools and processes.
Conduct thorough unit testing to ensure accuracy and reliability before QA handoff.
Manage individual projects independently, delivering on time with minimal oversight.
Troubleshoot reporting issues quickly and effectively, identifying root causes and implementing solutions.
Deepen your understanding of loyalty program metrics and contribute to their evolution.

What you need to be successful 
5+ years of hands-on SQL programming experience.
2+ years developing reports in SSRS (version 2008 R2 or later).
2+ years building dashboards in tools like Power BI, Tableau, QlikSense, or similar.
Experience with source control tools (e.g., BitBucket).
Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality.
Excellent communication skills—able to engage with clients, developers, and analysts.
Ability to performance tune SQL queries for efficiency.
Nice to have:
Bachelor’s degree preferred.
Familiarity with Snowflake and Star Schema design is a plus.
Experience applying AI to enhance reporting processes is a bonus.

\n
Who we are As a trusted partner, Kobie delivers market-leading, end-to-end loyalty solutions designed to enable customer experiences for the world's most successful brands. We do this with a strategy-led technology approach that uncovers the truth behind what drives consumers on an emotional level. We believe that our team's passion and expertise are the driving forces behind our success and are proud to be named a Top Workplaces in the USA, where the best and brightest in loyalty drive our mission of growing enterprise value through loyalty. 
A place for all We celebrate and embrace diversity at Kobie! 
Employment at Kobie is based solely on an individual's merit and qualifications, which are directly related to professional competence. We do not discriminate against any teammate or applicant because of race,color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. 
We are fiercely committed to fostering a workplace where teammates can bring their authentic selves to work every day. Our DEI initiatives, including various committees, ensure that principles of equity, diversity, and inclusion are deeply ingrained throughout Kobie. While our leadership team fully supports our policy of nondiscrimination and equal opportunity, it is the responsibility of all teammates to uphold these values. 
Ready to join us? If you’re ready to make an impact and grow in a supportive, innovative environment, we’d love to hear from you. Apply today and join the best and brightest in loyalty!
ai_core_responsibilities: As a BI Report Developer, you will design, develop, and maintain reports and dashboards while collaborating with data engineers and business stakeholders. You will also troubleshoot reporting issues and deepen your understanding of loyalty program metrics.
ai_requirements_summary: The position requires 5+ years of SQL programming experience and 2+ years of report development in SSRS. Familiarity with dashboard tools and source control is also necessary.

Kobie is a global, market-leading, end-to-end loyalty solutions provider for the world’s most successful brands. With strategy-led technology, Kobie is consistently named an industry leader by Forrester with a mission of growing value through loyalty.​
​
We turn complex customer data into actionable insights, leveraging composable technology, human-guided AI, and strategic services to enable loyalty outcomes across industries such as retail, travel and hospitality, financial services, entertainment, QSR, telecom and more.  Our Kobie Alchemy® Loyalty Cloud platform delivers and measures loyalty experiences that set brands apart.
 
Named a Top Workplace in the USA, and Top Remote Workplace, the best and brightest minds in loyalty come together at Kobie, driven by passion and innovation. 

We’re always looking for talented individuals, currently hiring in the U.S. and in India, who are ready to join a collaborative, growth-focused culture.
